name: Magic Kingdom
description: Magic Kingdom is the most iconic Walt Disney World theme park, home to Cinderella Castle and classic rides like Space Mountain, Pirates of the Caribbean, and the Haunted Mansion. The park draws over 20 million visitors per year, making it the most visited theme park on Earth.
transportNotes: Group bus pickup available from any Disney resort hotel or nearby Orlando area hotel. Early morning pickups for rope drop strategy and late-night returns after fireworks.
tips: Book buses for park-hopping days to avoid parking fees
Early morning pickups available for rope drop strategy

name: EPCOT
description: EPCOT celebrates human achievement and world culture with its iconic Spaceship Earth geodesic sphere, World Showcase pavilions representing 11 countries, and thrilling attractions like Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind. The park blends education and entertainment across two distinct areas.
transportNotes: Bus groups can be dropped at the main entrance or International Gateway for World Showcase access. Evening pickups timed to fireworks schedule.
tips: International Gateway entrance is closer to World Showcase countries
Festival seasons bring extra crowds - book early

name: Hollywood Studios
description: Hollywood Studios brings movies to life with immersive lands including Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge and Toy Story Land. The park features iconic attractions like Tower of Terror, Rock 'n' Roller Coaster, and Slinky Dog Dash.
transportNotes: Group drop-off at the main bus loop with dedicated charter parking. Galaxy's Edge draws heavy morning crowds so early pickups are recommended.
tips: Arrive early for Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge before crowds build
Fantasmic evening show is a great finale before bus pickup

name: Animal Kingdom
description: Animal Kingdom is Disney's largest theme park by area, combining a world-class zoo with thrilling rides like Expedition Everest and Avatar Flight of Passage in the immersive Pandora - The World of Avatar land. Kilimanjaro Safaris offers a real African safari experience.
transportNotes: Large bus parking area with easy group coordination. Morning pickups ideal for Kilimanjaro Safaris when animals are most active.
tips: Kilimanjaro Safaris is best in the morning when animals are active
Pandora is stunning at night - schedule a late pickup

name: Universal Studios Florida
description: Universal Studios Florida brings blockbuster movies to life with rides and attractions based on Harry Potter, Transformers, The Simpsons, and more. The Wizarding World of Harry Potter - Diagon Alley is a must-see immersive land connected to Islands of Adventure via the Hogwarts Express.
transportNotes: Charter buses drop off at the Universal transportation hub with easy access to both parks and CityWalk. Dedicated group parking available.
tips: Hogwarts Express connects both parks - a park-to-park ticket is recommended
CityWalk dining options available for group meals before pickup

name: Islands of Adventure
description: Islands of Adventure features some of Orlando's most thrilling rides including the Velocicoaster, Hagrid's Magical Creatures Motorbike Adventure, and the Incredible Hulk Coaster. The Wizarding World of Harry Potter - Hogsmeade was the original Harry Potter land.
transportNotes: Shares the Universal transportation hub with Universal Studios. Group coordination easy for multi-park days.
tips: Velocicoaster and Hagrid's draw the longest lines - arrive at park open
Park-to-park ticket needed for Hogwarts Express to Universal Studios

name: Universal Epic Universe
description: Universal's newest and most ambitious theme park features immersive worlds including Super Nintendo World, the Wizarding World of Harry Potter - Ministry of Magic, and How to Train Your Dragon. Epic Universe is Universal's third gate in Orlando.
transportNotes: Separate entrance from other Universal parks. Group bus staging area available at the new transportation hub.
tips: Expect heavy crowds during the opening year
Super Nintendo World is a must-see for gaming fans

name: SeaWorld Orlando
description: SeaWorld Orlando combines marine life exhibits with world-class roller coasters like Mako, Kraken, and Manta. The park features dolphin and sea lion shows, penguin encounters, and educational marine experiences alongside its thrill rides.
transportNotes: Easy bus access from International Drive hotels. Group parking lot with short walk to main gate.
tips: Coasters have shorter lines on weekdays
Check show schedules before planning your group visit

name: LEGOLAND Florida
description: LEGOLAND Florida is a 150-acre interactive theme park designed for families with children ages 2 to 12. The park features over 50 rides, shows, and attractions, plus Miniland USA with LEGO replicas of famous U.S. landmarks.
transportNotes: Located 45 minutes from Orlando - charter bus ideal for groups avoiding the drive. Dedicated bus drop-off area at the front gate.
tips: Best for families with kids under 12
Water park add-on available in warmer months

name: Busch Gardens Tampa Bay
description: Busch Gardens Tampa Bay is a 335-acre African-themed animal park and thrill ride destination featuring world-class coasters like SheiKra, Cheetah Hunt, and Iron Gwazi. The Serengeti Safari allows guests to get up close with free-roaming African animals.
transportNotes: Charter bus recommended for Orlando groups visiting Tampa. Large bus parking lot with group entry gate.
tips: Iron Gwazi is Florida's tallest hybrid coaster
Serengeti Safari is worth the add-on for animal lovers

name: Fun Spot America Orlando
description: Fun Spot America Orlando is a family-friendly amusement park on International Drive featuring go-karts, roller coasters, and a kid zone. The park offers free admission with pay-per-ride or wristband options.
transportNotes: Located on International Drive with easy bus access. Smaller venue good for quick group stops.
tips: Free admission - pay per ride or get a wristband
Great budget-friendly option for youth groups

name: Fun Spot America Kissimmee
description: Fun Spot America Kissimmee features multi-level go-kart tracks, thrill rides, and a family-friendly atmosphere near the US 192 tourist corridor. The park is a great affordable alternative to the major theme parks.
transportNotes: Easy bus access from US 192 corridor hotels. Good for shorter group outings.
tips: Located near other Kissimmee attractions for a combo day trip
Go-kart tracks are a group favorite

name: Disney's Typhoon Lagoon
address: 1145 E Buena Vista Blvd, Orlando, FL 32830
description: Typhoon Lagoon is a Disney water park themed around a tropical paradise hit by a storm, featuring one of the largest wave pools in the world. Attractions include Crush 'n' Gusher water coaster, Miss Adventure Falls family raft ride, and the Shark Reef snorkeling experience.
transportNotes: Bus drop-off at Disney's water park bus loop. Great add-on for groups with a half-day at a theme park.
tips: Wave pool is a must-try for the group
Seasonal closures - check schedule before booking

name: Disney's Blizzard Beach
description: Blizzard Beach is a Disney water park themed around a melting ski resort, featuring Summit Plummet - one of the tallest and fastest free-fall body slides in the world at 120 feet. The park offers slides, a lazy river, and a wave pool for all ages.
transportNotes: Disney bus loop access. Can be combined with nearby Animal Kingdom for a full day.
tips: Summit Plummet is not for the faint of heart
Seasonal closures apply - verify operating dates

name: Universal's Volcano Bay
description: Volcano Bay is Universal's tropical-themed water park centered around the Krakatau volcano, featuring the innovative TapuTapu wearable that lets guests virtually wait in line. Ko'okiri Body Plunge sends riders through a 70-degree drop through a trap door.
transportNotes: Part of the Universal Resort complex. Buses drop at the Universal hub with a short walk to Volcano Bay entrance.
tips: TapuTapu virtual queue means less standing in line
Arrive early for best ride availability

name: Adventure Island
description: Adventure Island is a 30-acre water park adjacent to Busch Gardens Tampa Bay, featuring water slides, a wave pool, and a lazy river. The park is a perfect add-on for groups visiting Busch Gardens.
transportNotes: Adjacent to Busch Gardens - same bus parking area works for both. Combo day trips popular for groups.
tips: Combo tickets with Busch Gardens save money
Seasonal operation - open spring through fall

name: Aquatica Orlando
description: Aquatica is SeaWorld's water park featuring high-speed slides, a wave pool, lazy river, and unique animal encounters including a slide that passes through a pool of dolphins. The park blends marine life with water park thrills.
transportNotes: Located next to SeaWorld on International Drive. Easy combo with SeaWorld for a full group day.
tips: Dolphin Plunge slide is a must-try
Less crowded than Disney or Universal water parks

name: Kennedy Space Center Visitor Complex
address: Space Commerce Way, Merritt Island, FL 32953
description: Kennedy Space Center Visitor Complex offers an immersive look at NASA's past, present, and future with exhibits including the Space Shuttle Atlantis, Saturn V rocket, and launch viewing opportunities. The Astronaut Training Experience lets visitors train like a real astronaut.
transportNotes: About one hour east of Orlando. Charter bus is the best option for groups - large bus lot with group check-in area.
tips: Book the Astronaut Training Experience in advance for groups
Check the launch schedule - watching a real launch is unforgettable

name: Orlando Science Center
description: Orlando Science Center is a hands-on science museum featuring four floors of interactive exhibits, a planetarium, an observatory, and rotating special exhibitions. The museum is a popular destination for school groups and families.
transportNotes: Located in downtown Orlando's Loch Haven Park. Street-side bus drop-off available with nearby parking.
tips: Perfect for school groups and corporate team-building
Check for special evening events and Science Night Live

name: Museum of Science and Industry (MOSI)
description: MOSI is Tampa's premier science center featuring interactive exhibits, a planetarium, and rotating special exhibitions covering topics from space exploration to the human body. The museum offers hands-on STEM learning for all ages.
transportNotes: Located near USF campus in Tampa. Easy bus access with dedicated parking lot.
tips: Great for school field trips and STEM-focused groups
Planetarium shows are included with admission

name: The Dali Museum
description: The Dali Museum houses the world's most comprehensive collection of Salvador Dali's work, featuring over 2,400 objects including paintings, sculptures, and photographs. The building itself is an architectural masterpiece on the St. Petersburg waterfront.
transportNotes: Waterfront location in downtown St. Pete. Bus drop-off on Dali Blvd with nearby lot parking.
tips: Guided group tours available with advance booking
The building architecture is as impressive as the art inside

name: The Ringling Museum
description: The John and Mable Ringling Museum of Art is a 66-acre estate featuring a world-class art museum, the Circus Museum, historic Ca' d'Zan mansion, and beautiful bayfront gardens. The collection includes European paintings, Asian art, and circus memorabilia.
transportNotes: Located on Sarasota Bay. Large parking lot accommodates buses with group entrance coordination.
tips: Allow at least 3 hours for the full estate experience
Ca' d'Zan mansion tour is a highlight

name: NSU Art Museum Fort Lauderdale
address: 1 E Las Olas Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33301
description: NSU Art Museum Fort Lauderdale is one of the largest art museums in Florida, featuring an extensive collection of contemporary art in a striking building designed by Edward Larrabee Barnes. The museum sits on Las Olas Boulevard in the heart of downtown.
transportNotes: Downtown Fort Lauderdale location with bus drop-off on Las Olas Blvd. Walking distance to restaurants and shopping.
tips: Combine with a Las Olas Boulevard shopping and dining walk
Free guided tours available for groups with advance notice

name: Norton Museum of Art
address: 1450 S Dixie Hwy, West Palm Beach, FL 33401
description: The Norton Museum of Art is one of South Florida's premier cultural institutions, featuring an impressive collection of American, European, Chinese, and contemporary art. The recently expanded museum campus includes sculpture gardens and a new wing.
transportNotes: Easy bus access on South Dixie Highway. Group visits can be arranged with advance booking.
tips: Free admission on certain days - check the schedule
Sculpture garden is a peaceful group photo spot

name: Henry B. Flagler Museum
description: Whitehall, the Flagler Museum, is a 75-room Gilded Age mansion built by Standard Oil co-founder Henry Flagler in 1902. The museum showcases Flagler's role in developing Florida's east coast and features opulent period rooms and Flagler's personal railcar.
transportNotes: Located on Palm Beach island. Bus parking available on Cocoanut Row with a short walk to the entrance.
tips: Book group tours in advance for docent-led experience
The private railcar exhibit is a highlight

name: Lightner Museum
description: The Lightner Museum is housed in the former Alcazar Hotel, built by Henry Flagler in 1888. The museum features collections of Gilded Age art, antiques, and curiosities including Tiffany glass, Victorian-era science instruments, and a stunning indoor swimming pool turned gallery.
transportNotes: Located in historic downtown St. Augustine. Bus drop-off on King Street with group parking nearby.
tips: Combine with a walking tour of historic St. Augustine
The former indoor pool gallery is a must-see

name: Cummer Museum of Art and Gardens
description: The Cummer Museum houses over 5,000 works of art spanning from 2100 BC to the present, plus 2.5 acres of historic gardens on the St. Johns River. The museum is one of the finest in Northeast Florida.
transportNotes: Riverside location with bus parking along Riverside Ave. Group tours available.
tips: The riverside gardens are beautiful for group photos
Free admission on certain days

name: Charles Hosmer Morse Museum of American Art
address: 445 N Park Ave, Winter Park, FL 32789
description: The Morse Museum holds the world's most comprehensive collection of works by Louis Comfort Tiffany, including the stunning Tiffany Chapel from the 1893 World's Columbian Exposition. The museum is a hidden gem on Winter Park's charming Park Avenue.
transportNotes: Located on Park Avenue in Winter Park. Street-side bus drop-off with nearby lot parking.
tips: The Tiffany Chapel is the centerpiece - do not miss it
Combine with a stroll down Park Avenue for dining and shopping

name: Tampa Museum of Art
description: The Tampa Museum of Art features a growing collection of contemporary and classical art in a striking LED-lit waterfront building along the Tampa Riverwalk. The museum hosts rotating exhibitions and community events.
transportNotes: Located on the Tampa Riverwalk. Bus drop-off at Gasparilla Plaza with nearby parking garages.
tips: The building is stunning at night with LED lighting
Walk the Riverwalk before or after your visit

name: Brevard Zoo
description: Brevard Zoo is home to over 900 animals from around the world, featuring kayaking through animal habitats, a zip line, and a rhino encounter experience. The zoo is consistently rated one of the best in the southeastern United States.
transportNotes: Located in Melbourne, about an hour east of Orlando. Bus parking available at the front entrance.
tips: Kayak through the animal exhibits for a unique experience
Rhino encounter is bookable for small groups

name: Central Florida Zoo and Botanical Gardens
description: Central Florida Zoo and Botanical Gardens is home to over 400 animals on 116 acres in Sanford, featuring a splash park, zip line, and aerial adventure course. The zoo offers a more intimate animal experience compared to larger parks.
transportNotes: Located near I-4 in Sanford. Bus parking available with group entrance coordination.
tips: Great alternative to bigger parks for younger groups
Splash park is a hit with kids during hot months

name: ZooTampa at Lowry Park
description: ZooTampa at Lowry Park is home to over 1,300 animals and has been voted the number one zoo in the U.S. by multiple publications. The zoo features a Florida manatee rehabilitation center, African elephant habitat, and immersive animal encounters.
transportNotes: North Tampa location with dedicated bus parking. Group rates and guided tours available.
tips: Manatee viewing area is especially popular in winter
Book group catering for a full-day zoo experience

name: Clearwater Marine Aquarium
description: Clearwater Marine Aquarium is a marine rescue center famous as the home of Winter the dolphin from the Dolphin Tale movies. The facility focuses on rescue, rehabilitation, and release of marine animals while educating visitors about ocean conservation.
transportNotes: Located on Clearwater Beach Island. Bus drop-off at the main entrance with nearby lot parking.
tips: Great educational experience for school and youth groups
Boat tours available for dolphin watching in the bay

name: The Florida Aquarium
description: The Florida Aquarium is a 250,000 square foot aquarium in downtown Tampa featuring over 14,000 aquatic plants and animals. Highlights include a coral reef gallery, penguin habitat, shark encounters, and a Wild Dolphin Cruise on Tampa Bay.
transportNotes: Channelside location in downtown Tampa. Bus drop-off area with nearby parking garages.
tips: Wild Dolphin Cruise is a popular group add-on
Located near other Tampa attractions for a full day trip

name: SEA LIFE Orlando Aquarium
description: SEA LIFE Orlando is an indoor aquarium on International Drive featuring a 360-degree ocean tunnel, touch pools, and thousands of sea creatures. The aquarium is connected to LEGOLAND Discovery Center and Madame Tussauds.
transportNotes: Located at ICON Park on International Drive. Easy bus access from I-Drive hotels.
tips: Combo tickets available with ICON Park attractions
Good rainy day option for groups

name: Gatorland
address: 14501 S Orange Blossom Trail, Orlando, FL 32837
description: Gatorland is the 'Alligator Capital of the World' featuring thousands of alligators and crocodiles on 110 acres. The park offers zip lining over gator ponds, the Screamin' Gator Zip Line, and gator wrestling shows that have entertained visitors since 1949.
transportNotes: Located on South Orange Blossom Trail. Bus parking with group pricing available.
tips: Zip line over gators is a unique group activity
One of Orlando's oldest attractions - great for a half-day trip

name: Cocoa Beach
description: Cocoa Beach is a popular Atlantic coast beach town known for surfing, the famous Cocoa Beach Pier, and Ron Jon Surf Shop. The beach is the closest ocean beach to Orlando and offers miles of sandy shoreline.
transportNotes: About one hour east of Orlando. Bus drop-off areas near the Cocoa Beach Pier and public beach access points.
tips: Visit Ron Jon Surf Shop for souvenirs
Great day trip from Orlando - combine with Kennedy Space Center

name: Clearwater Beach
description: Clearwater Beach is consistently rated one of the best beaches in the United States, featuring sugar-white sand and calm, clear Gulf waters. The beachfront promenade offers dining, shopping, and nightly sunset celebrations at Pier 60.
transportNotes: About 90 minutes from Orlando. Bus staging areas near the roundabout and public beach parking lots.
tips: Pier 60 sunset celebration is a must for groups
Arrive early for best beach positioning

name: Daytona Beach
description: Daytona Beach is famous for its hard-packed sand where cars are still allowed to drive on the beach. The Daytona Beach Boardwalk and Pier offer classic seaside entertainment, and the area is known worldwide for the Daytona 500.
transportNotes: About one hour northeast of Orlando. Bus access via Atlantic Avenue and beach approach ramps.
tips: Cars can still drive on parts of the beach - unique Florida experience
Boardwalk area great for group entertainment

name: New Smyrna Beach
description: New Smyrna Beach is a charming coastal town known for excellent surfing, a vibrant arts district on Flagler Avenue, and a more laid-back atmosphere compared to Daytona Beach. The town consistently ranks among the best small beach towns in Florida.
transportNotes: About 75 minutes from Orlando. Bus parking near Flagler Avenue and beach access points.
tips: Flagler Avenue has great restaurants for group dining
Less crowded alternative to Daytona Beach

name: Siesta Key Beach
description: Siesta Key Beach features the finest, whitest quartz sand in the world, so pure it stays cool even on the hottest days. The beach has been named the number one beach in the United States multiple times by Dr. Beach.
transportNotes: About two hours from Orlando. Bus parking at the public beach lot on Beach Road.
tips: The sand is 99% pure quartz - unlike any other beach
Siesta Key Village nearby for group dining

name: St. Pete Beach
description: St. Pete Beach is a barrier island on the Gulf of Mexico featuring wide white sand beaches, the iconic Don CeSar pink palace hotel, and consistently warm, calm waters. The beach is a TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice winner.
transportNotes: About 90 minutes from Orlando. Bus access via Gulf Boulevard with public lot parking.
tips: The Don CeSar is a landmark worth seeing even if not staying there
Gulf waters are calmer and warmer than Atlantic side

name: Fort Lauderdale Beach
description: Fort Lauderdale Beach stretches along the iconic A1A highway with its signature wave wall, palm-lined promenade, and turquoise Atlantic waters. The beach is a major spring break and tourism destination with waterfront restaurants and bars.
transportNotes: Bus drop-off areas along A1A. Multiple public parking lots for charter bus staging.
tips: The promenade is perfect for group walks
Water taxi available for sightseeing along the Intracoastal

name: Deerfield Beach
description: Deerfield Beach is a Blue Wave certified beach known for its International Fishing Pier, calm waters, and family-friendly atmosphere. The beach town offers a quieter alternative to neighboring Fort Lauderdale and Boca Raton.
transportNotes: Easy bus access via A1A. Public parking lots near the pier for bus staging.
tips: International Fishing Pier is a local landmark
Quieter beach option for groups wanting less crowds

name: Jupiter Beach
description: Jupiter Beach is a beautiful stretch of coastline anchored by the iconic Jupiter Inlet Lighthouse, one of Florida's oldest lighthouses. The beach area offers natural sea turtle nesting habitat and excellent snorkeling at the nearby reef.
transportNotes: Bus parking available at Carlin Park and Jupiter Beach Park. About 90 minutes from Orlando.
tips: Jupiter Lighthouse tours available for groups
Sea turtle nesting season runs May through October

name: Vero Beach
description: Vero Beach is an upscale beach community on Florida's Treasure Coast known for pristine beaches, excellent art galleries, and historic downtown. The area is also home to Disney's Vero Beach Resort and McKee Botanical Garden.
transportNotes: About two hours from Orlando. Bus access via the A1A bridge to the barrier island beaches.
tips: McKee Botanical Garden is a great add-on stop
Less touristy than other Florida beach towns

name: Melbourne Beach
description: Melbourne Beach is a quiet barrier island beach community on the Space Coast featuring uncrowded shoreline, sea turtle nesting areas, and the Sebastian Inlet at its southern end. The town maintains a small-town feel away from the tourist crowds.
transportNotes: About 75 minutes from Orlando. Limited bus staging but public beach access points available.
tips: Sebastian Inlet at the south end is great for surfing and fishing
Much less crowded than Cocoa Beach

name: Canaveral National Seashore
description: Canaveral National Seashore is a 24-mile stretch of undeveloped barrier island beach preserved as a national park. The seashore protects pristine beaches, sea turtle nesting habitat, and the Mosquito Lagoon - one of the best kayaking and fishing spots in Florida.
transportNotes: About 75 minutes from Orlando. Bus access via the north or south entrance. Vehicle entrance fee applies.
tips: Completely undeveloped beach - bring everything you need
Mosquito Lagoon kayaking is world-class

name: Blue Spring State Park
address: 2100 W French Ave, Orange City, FL 32763
description: Blue Spring State Park is a first-magnitude spring and one of the top manatee viewing spots in Florida, with hundreds of manatees gathering in the crystal-clear 72-degree spring run during winter months. The park offers swimming, kayaking, and nature trails.
transportNotes: About 45 minutes north of Orlando. Bus parking at the main lot with group entrance.
tips: November through March is peak manatee season
Swimming is available in warmer months when manatees are not present

name: Wekiwa Springs State Park
description: Wekiwa Springs State Park is a natural oasis just north of Orlando featuring a crystal-clear natural spring for swimming, miles of hiking and biking trails, and kayak and canoe access to the Wekiva River. The park is a popular escape from the theme park crowds.
transportNotes: Only 30 minutes north of Orlando. Bus parking at the main entrance lot.
tips: Spring water is a constant 72 degrees year-round
Canoe and kayak rentals available on-site

name: De Leon Springs State Park
address: 601 Ponce De Leon Blvd, De Leon Springs, FL 32130
description: De Leon Springs State Park features a natural spring-fed swimming area and the unique Old Spanish Sugar Mill Grill where visitors cook their own pancakes on griddles built into the tables. The park offers kayaking, hiking, and eco-boat tours.
transportNotes: About one hour north of Orlando. Bus parking available with group reservations recommended for the restaurant.
tips: Cook-your-own pancakes at the Old Spanish Sugar Mill is a unique experience
Book restaurant reservations in advance for groups

name: Crystal River Manatee Tours
description: Crystal River is the only place in Florida where you can legally swim with wild manatees in their natural habitat. Three Sisters Springs and Kings Bay attract hundreds of manatees during winter months, offering unforgettable snorkeling encounters.
transportNotes: About 90 minutes northwest of Orlando. Charter bus recommended for groups. Multiple tour operators offer bus-friendly staging.
tips: Winter months (November-March) are best for manatee encounters
Book tour boat in advance for groups

name: Bok Tower Gardens
description: Bok Tower Gardens is a 250-acre garden and bird sanctuary featuring the 205-foot Singing Tower carillon with daily bell concerts. Designed by Frederick Law Olmsted Jr., the gardens sit atop Iron Mountain, one of the highest points in peninsular Florida.
transportNotes: About 60 minutes south of Orlando. Bus parking at the main entrance with group rates.
tips: Carillon concerts happen daily at 1pm and 3pm
Pinewood Estate tour is a beautiful add-on

name: Harry P. Leu Gardens
description: Harry P. Leu Gardens is a 50-acre botanical oasis in the heart of Orlando featuring one of the largest camellia collections in the eastern U.S., a tropical stream garden, butterfly garden, and the historic Leu House Museum.
transportNotes: Located minutes from downtown Orlando. Bus parking at the main lot off Forest Avenue.
tips: Popular wedding venue - transport groups for ceremony events
Free admission on the first Monday of each month

name: Mead Botanical Garden
address: 1300 S Denning Dr, Winter Park, FL 32789
description: Mead Botanical Garden is a 47-acre urban nature retreat in Winter Park featuring native Florida habitats, butterfly gardens, and walking trails. The garden hosts community events, concerts, and nature education programs throughout the year.
transportNotes: Located in Winter Park, 15 minutes from downtown Orlando. Street parking with bus drop-off on Denning Drive.
tips: Free admission makes it a budget-friendly group stop
Combine with a visit to nearby Park Avenue shops

name: Everglades Day Safari
description: Everglades Day Safari offers guided day trips into the Everglades National Park from the Fort Myers area, including airboat rides, nature walks, and wildlife viewing. The tours provide access to one of the most unique ecosystems in the world.
transportNotes: Full-day trip from the Tampa or Orlando area. Charter bus brings groups to the safari launch point with all transport included.
tips: Full-day excursion - plan for 8+ hours
Dry season (December-April) is the best time for wildlife viewing

name: ICON Park
description: ICON Park is an entertainment complex on International Drive anchored by The Wheel, a 400-foot observation wheel offering panoramic views of Central Florida. The complex includes Madame Tussauds, SEA LIFE Aquarium, museums, restaurants, and attractions.
transportNotes: Central I-Drive location with dedicated bus drop-off area. Walking distance from many I-Drive hotels.
tips: The Wheel is best at sunset for photos
Multiple attractions can fill a full evening for groups

name: The Wheel at ICON Park
description: The Wheel at ICON Park is a 400-foot tall observation wheel - the tallest on the East Coast - offering breathtaking views of Orlando's skyline, theme parks, and on clear days, both coasts of Florida. Each climate-controlled capsule holds up to 15 guests.
transportNotes: Part of ICON Park on International Drive. Bus drop-off at the ICON Park entrance.
tips: Sunset rides offer the best photo opportunities
Group capsule bookings available for private experiences

name: Madame Tussauds Orlando
description: Madame Tussauds Orlando features lifelike wax figures of celebrities, historical figures, sports stars, and pop culture icons across themed interactive zones. The attraction encourages visitors to pose and interact with the figures for photos.
transportNotes: Located at ICON Park on International Drive. Part of the ICON Park bus drop-off zone.
tips: Combo tickets with The Wheel and SEA LIFE save money
Fun group activity - especially for photo lovers

name: WonderWorks Orlando
description: WonderWorks is an interactive science attraction housed in an upside-down building on International Drive, featuring over 100 hands-on exhibits covering natural disasters, space, physical challenges, and illusions. The venue also hosts a laser tag arena and ropes course.
transportNotes: I-Drive location with bus drop-off at the front entrance. Group rates and packages available.
tips: Great rainy day or evening activity for groups
Laser tag and ropes course are popular add-ons

name: Andretti Indoor Karting and Games
description: Andretti Indoor Karting and Games is a premium entertainment complex featuring high-speed indoor go-karts on a multi-level track, bowling, arcade games, zip lining, and a full restaurant and bar. The venue is ideal for corporate events and group outings.
transportNotes: Located on Universal Blvd near the convention center. Bus parking with group event coordinator on-site.
tips: Book group event packages for the best rates
Multi-level go-kart track is a thrilling group activity

name: Topgolf Orlando
description: Topgolf Orlando is a premier entertainment venue combining golf, food, and fun with climate-controlled hitting bays across three levels. The venue features a rooftop terrace, full restaurant, and event spaces perfect for corporate outings.
transportNotes: Located on Universal Blvd. Bus drop-off at the main entrance with nearby parking.
tips: Reserve bays in advance for large groups
Great corporate team-building activity

name: Dave and Buster's Orlando
description: Dave and Buster's on International Drive combines a full-service restaurant with an enormous arcade featuring hundreds of games, virtual reality experiences, and billiards. The venue is a popular group dining and entertainment destination.
transportNotes: I-Drive location with easy bus access. Group event packages available with advance booking.
tips: Group event packages include food, drinks, and game play
Great evening activity after a day at the parks

name: Disney Springs
description: Disney Springs is Walt Disney World's premier dining, shopping, and entertainment district featuring over 150 shops and restaurants. Highlights include the NBA Experience, Cirque du Soleil, World of Disney store, and dozens of unique dining options.
transportNotes: Free parking and dedicated bus drop-off areas. No theme park admission required - easy group access.
tips: No admission fee - just show up and enjoy
Dinner reservations recommended for groups at popular restaurants

name: Universal CityWalk
description: Universal CityWalk is the dining, shopping, and nightlife hub connecting Universal's theme parks. The complex features restaurants like Toothsome Chocolate Emporium, live music venues, a movie theater, and the Blue Man Group show.
transportNotes: Part of the Universal Resort transportation hub. Buses drop at the main hub with a short walk to CityWalk.
tips: No admission fee to CityWalk - great for evening group dining
Live music and entertainment most evenings

name: International Drive
description: International Drive is Orlando's main tourist corridor stretching over 10 miles with hundreds of restaurants, shops, attractions, and hotels. The I-Drive Trolley provides easy transportation along the strip, and the area is home to ICON Park, outlet malls, and dinner shows.
transportNotes: Central corridor for most Orlando group travel. Bus access at virtually every point along the road.
tips: I-Drive Trolley is an affordable way to move small groups
Orlando Premium Outlets are at the south end

name: Tampa Riverwalk
description: The Tampa Riverwalk is a 2.6-mile pedestrian trail along the Hillsborough River connecting downtown Tampa's cultural attractions, parks, restaurants, and entertainment venues. The Riverwalk connects the Florida Aquarium, Tampa Museum of Art, and Armature Works.
transportNotes: Multiple bus drop-off points along the Riverwalk route. Downtown parking garages available for buses.
tips: Walk connects most major Tampa attractions
Water bike and boat rentals available along the Riverwalk

name: Las Olas Boulevard
address: Las Olas Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33301
description: Las Olas Boulevard is Fort Lauderdale's most iconic shopping and dining street, stretching from downtown to the beach. The tree-lined boulevard features boutiques, galleries, sidewalk cafes, and some of the best restaurants in South Florida.
transportNotes: Bus drop-off at either end of Las Olas. The boulevard connects downtown to the beach for easy group walking.
tips: Walk from downtown to the beach for the full Las Olas experience
Gallery Night events on the first Friday of the month

name: Worth Avenue
description: Worth Avenue is one of the most exclusive shopping streets in the world, featuring luxury boutiques, designer brands, art galleries, and fine dining on the island of Palm Beach. The Mediterranean-style architecture and hidden courtyards make it a destination in itself.
transportNotes: Bus drop-off on South County Road. Limited parking on the island - charter bus is the best option for groups.
tips: Explore the hidden vias (alleyways) for unique shops and galleries
Architecture and window shopping alone are worth the trip

name: Sawgrass Mills
description: Sawgrass Mills is the largest single-story outlet and value retail shopping destination in the United States with over 350 stores. The mall includes The Colonnade Outlets featuring luxury brands at outlet prices alongside dining and entertainment options.
transportNotes: Large bus parking area with group coordination. About 30 minutes from Fort Lauderdale hotels.
tips: Plan at least 3-4 hours for shopping groups
The Colonnade Outlets section has the luxury brands

name: Orlando Vineland Premium Outlets
description: Orlando Vineland Premium Outlets is a massive outdoor outlet mall near Disney World with over 160 designer and name brand stores. The shopping center is one of the most visited outlet destinations in the world.
transportNotes: Located near Disney Springs with bus parking available. Popular stop for international tour groups.
tips: Near Disney Springs - can combine both in one outing
Very popular with international visitors - can be crowded

name: Orange County Convention Center
description: The Orange County Convention Center is the second-largest convention center in the United States at 7 million square feet, hosting major trade shows, conferences, and consumer events on International Drive. The center handles over 200 events annually.
transportNotes: Dedicated bus staging areas at both the West and North-South buildings. Group transportation is essential for major events.
tips: Two buildings connected by a skybridge - confirm which building your event is in
I-Drive hotels are within walking or short shuttle distance

name: Tampa Convention Center
description: The Tampa Convention Center is a 600,000 square foot waterfront facility in downtown Tampa hosting conventions, trade shows, and special events. Its location on the Tampa Riverwalk provides easy access to downtown hotels and restaurants.
transportNotes: Downtown Tampa waterfront location. Bus drop-off on Franklin Street with nearby parking garages.
tips: Waterfront location near Tampa Riverwalk restaurants
Book hotel shuttles for multi-day conventions

name: Ocean Center
address: 101 N Atlantic Ave, Daytona Beach, FL 32118
description: The Ocean Center is Daytona Beach's premier convention and entertainment venue located directly on the beachside, hosting concerts, conventions, trade shows, and sporting events in over 200,000 square feet of flexible space.
transportNotes: Beachside location with bus drop-off on Atlantic Avenue. Walking distance to boardwalk hotels.
tips: Beachfront location is a bonus for event attendees
Connected to the Hilton Daytona Beach Oceanfront Resort

name: Broward County Convention Center
description: The Broward County Convention Center is a 600,000 square foot facility adjacent to Port Everglades, hosting conventions, trade shows, and events. A major expansion is underway to make it one of the premier convention destinations in the Southeast.
transportNotes: Located near Port Everglades and Fort Lauderdale-Hollywood International Airport. Bus staging area available.
tips: Close to airport and cruise port for combo travel groups
Fort Lauderdale Beach is minutes away

name: Palm Beach County Convention Center
address: 650 Okeechobee Blvd, West Palm Beach, FL 33401
description: The Palm Beach County Convention Center offers 350,000 square feet of meeting and exhibition space in downtown West Palm Beach. The center hosts trade shows, conventions, and community events with easy access to CityPlace and Clematis Street entertainment.
transportNotes: Downtown West Palm Beach location. Bus drop-off on Okeechobee Boulevard with parking garage access.
tips: CityPlace dining and entertainment within walking distance
Connected to the Hilton West Palm Beach

name: Camping World Stadium
description: Camping World Stadium is a 65,000-seat multi-purpose stadium in downtown Orlando hosting college football bowl games, the NFL Pro Bowl, major concerts, and Orlando City SC soccer matches. The stadium is a major event venue for Central Florida.
transportNotes: Downtown Orlando location with dedicated bus staging lots for major events. Group transportation essential for game days.
tips: Book buses early for bowl games and major concerts
Tailgating lots available for groups

name: Amway Center
description: Amway Center is a 20,000-seat arena in downtown Orlando and home to the NBA's Orlando Magic. The arena hosts concerts, Disney on Ice, UFC events, and other major entertainment events throughout the year.
transportNotes: Downtown Orlando with nearby parking garages. Bus drop-off on Church Street with event-day traffic coordination.
tips: Downtown location means easy pre-game dining options
Book return pickup after events to avoid surge pricing

name: Raymond James Stadium
address: 4201 N Dale Mabry Hwy, Tampa, FL 33607
description: Raymond James Stadium is a 75,000-seat stadium home to the NFL's Tampa Bay Buccaneers, featuring the iconic pirate ship that fires cannons after touchdowns. The stadium has hosted multiple Super Bowls, college football championships, and major concerts.
transportNotes: Large bus staging areas for game days and events. Charter bus is the best option for groups attending games.
tips: Book buses well in advance for NFL game days
Tailgate lots fill up fast - bus transport avoids parking hassle

name: Tropicana Field
description: Tropicana Field is the domed stadium home to the MLB's Tampa Bay Rays in St. Petersburg. The unique indoor ballpark offers baseball rain or shine and hosts concerts and events during the off-season.
transportNotes: Large parking lots surround the stadium with bus-friendly access. About 30 minutes from downtown Tampa.
tips: Indoor dome means no weather worries
Group ticket and bus packages available for corporate outings

name: Daytona International Speedway
address: 1801 W International Speedway Blvd, Daytona Beach, FL 32114
description: Daytona International Speedway is the 'World Center of Racing' and home to the Daytona 500, the most prestigious race in NASCAR. The recently renovated facility features the DAYTONA Rising experience with premium seating, hospitality, and year-round tours.
transportNotes: Massive bus staging areas for race events. Charter bus is essential for race day groups to avoid traffic and parking.
tips: Book buses months in advance for Daytona 500 week
Speedway tours available year-round when no events are scheduled

name: Homestead-Miami Speedway
description: Homestead-Miami Speedway is a major motorsports venue hosting NASCAR Cup Series races, IndyCar, and other racing events. The speedway also offers driving experiences where guests can drive stock cars on the track.
transportNotes: Located south of Miami. Charter bus from Fort Lauderdale or Miami hotels recommended for race events.
tips: Stock car driving experiences available for corporate groups
Plan for traffic on race days - bus beats driving

name: Hard Rock Stadium
address: 347 Don Shula Dr, Miami Gardens, FL 33056
description: Hard Rock Stadium is a 65,000-seat multi-purpose stadium home to the NFL's Miami Dolphins and host of the Miami Open tennis tournament, Formula 1 Miami Grand Prix, and major college football games including the Orange Bowl.
transportNotes: Bus staging areas for events. Charter bus recommended from Fort Lauderdale and Miami hotels.
tips: Hosts F1, NFL, tennis, and college football - check event calendar
Bus avoids the significant event-day traffic

name: Inter and Co Stadium
description: Inter and Co Stadium is the home of Inter Miami CF, the MLS team co-owned by David Beckham. The 21,550-seat soccer-specific stadium hosts MLS matches and international soccer events in Fort Lauderdale.
transportNotes: Located in Fort Lauderdale. Bus parking and group drop-off available on event days.
tips: Inter Miami matches draw big crowds - book transport early
Pre-match dining options limited near stadium - eat before arriving

name: Ybor City Historic District
description: Ybor City is Tampa's historic Latin Quarter, founded by cigar manufacturers in the 1880s. The National Historic Landmark District features cobblestone streets, historic architecture, Columbia Restaurant (Florida's oldest), and a vibrant nightlife scene.
transportNotes: Downtown Tampa location. Bus drop-off on 7th or 8th Avenue with nearby parking lots.
tips: Columbia Restaurant is a must for group dining - book ahead
The Centro Ybor complex has entertainment and shopping

name: St. Augustine Historic District
description: St. Augustine is the oldest continuously occupied European settlement in the United States, founded in 1565. The historic district features the Castillo de San Marcos, Flagler College, the Spanish Quarter, and centuries of preserved architecture.
transportNotes: About 2 hours from Orlando. Bus parking at the visitor center with trolley access to all historic sites.
tips: Full day needed to see the major historic sites
Trolley tours are a great way for groups to see everything

name: Cassadaga Spiritualist Camp
description: Cassadaga is a small unincorporated community established in 1894 as a spiritualist camp. Known as the 'Psychic Capital of the World,' the community offers psychic readings, mediumship sessions, spiritual healing, and ghost tours in a unique historic setting.
transportNotes: About 45 minutes north of Orlando. Small community with limited bus staging but manageable for smaller groups.
tips: Book readings in advance as the most popular mediums fill up fast
The Cassadaga Hotel offers group tours

name: Coral Castle Museum
description: Coral Castle is an oolite limestone structure created single-handedly by Latvian-American Edward Leedskalnin over 28 years. The mysterious engineering feat, with stones weighing up to 30 tons, remains one of Florida's most unique historical curiosities.
transportNotes: Located in Homestead, south of Miami. Bus parking on South Dixie Highway.
tips: The mystery of how one man moved these stones is still debated
Tours are self-guided with audio available

name: Edison and Ford Winter Estates
description: The Edison and Ford Winter Estates preserve the winter homes and botanical gardens of Thomas Edison and Henry Ford along the Caloosahatchee River. The museum showcases Edison's inventions, Ford's automobiles, and a stunning collection of tropical plants.
transportNotes: Located on McGregor Blvd in Fort Myers. Bus parking with group entrance available.
tips: Allow 2-3 hours for the full estate and museum tour
The botanical gardens are world-class

name: Glazer Children's Museum
description: The Glazer Children's Museum is a hands-on museum in downtown Tampa designed for children ages 0-10. Interactive exhibits cover art, science, engineering, and community with a focus on learning through play on the Tampa Riverwalk.
transportNotes: Located on the Tampa Riverwalk next to the Tampa Museum of Art. Bus drop-off at Gasparilla Plaza.
tips: Perfect for school groups and young families
Combine with other Riverwalk attractions for a full day

name: Orlando Eye (The Wheel)
description: The Wheel at ICON Park (formerly the Orlando Eye) provides sweeping 400-foot-high views of Orlando and Central Florida from enclosed, air-conditioned capsules. On clear days, visibility extends to both the Atlantic and Gulf coasts.
transportNotes: Located at ICON Park on International Drive with bus drop-off access.
tips: Clear days offer the best views of both coasts
Champagne flight upgrade available for groups

name: Discovery Cove
description: Discovery Cove is an all-inclusive day resort by SeaWorld where guests can swim with dolphins, snorkel with tropical fish and rays, hand-feed exotic birds, and relax on pristine beaches. Daily attendance is limited for an exclusive experience.
transportNotes: Located near SeaWorld on International Drive. Dedicated bus entrance with group check-in.
tips: All-inclusive pricing includes meals, drinks, and gear
Limited daily attendance - book months in advance for groups

name: LEGOLAND Water Park
description: LEGOLAND Water Park is an add-on water park at LEGOLAND Florida featuring water slides, a lazy river, and interactive water play areas - all with a LEGO theme. The Build-A-Raft River lets kids customize their own LEGO raft.
transportNotes: Same location as LEGOLAND Florida in Winter Haven. Same bus parking and group entrance.
tips: Combo ticket with LEGOLAND theme park saves money
Build-A-Raft River is a unique group favorite

name: Museum of Illusions Orlando
description: The Museum of Illusions Orlando features mind-bending rooms and optical illusions that trick your eyes and challenge your perceptions. The interactive experience is perfect for all ages and creates amazing photo opportunities.
transportNotes: Located at ICON Park on International Drive. Easy bus access from I-Drive hotels.
tips: Great photo opportunity for groups
Allow about an hour for the full experience

name: Ripley's Believe It or Not! Orlando
description: Ripley's Believe It or Not! Odditorium in Orlando is housed in a building that appears to be sinking into the ground. Inside, visitors find bizarre artifacts, interactive exhibits, and strange-but-true curiosities from around the world.
transportNotes: I-Drive location with bus drop-off at the main entrance.
tips: The tilting building is a photo op in itself
Fun evening activity for groups on I-Drive

name: Titanic: The Artifact Exhibition
description: Titanic: The Artifact Exhibition on International Drive features over 300 artifacts recovered from the Titanic wreck site, full-scale room recreations, and interactive experiences. Each visitor receives a boarding pass of an actual Titanic passenger.
transportNotes: I-Drive location with easy bus access. Group tours with actors in period costume available.
tips: Interactive dinner theater experience available for groups
Allow 1-2 hours for the full exhibit

name: Crayola Experience Orlando
address: 8001 S Orange Blossom Trail, Orlando, FL 32809
description: The Crayola Experience Orlando is a 70,000 square foot family attraction at the Florida Mall featuring 25 hands-on creative activities including naming your own crayon color, starring in a coloring page, and sculpting with melted wax.
transportNotes: Located at Florida Mall. Bus access via the mall bus parking area.
tips: Great for younger groups and family events
Name and wrap your own crayon as a souvenir

name: Dezerland Park Orlando
description: Dezerland Park is a 900,000 square foot indoor entertainment complex on International Drive featuring go-karts, bowling, an auto museum with over 2,000 vehicles, trampolines, and an arcade. It is one of the largest indoor attractions in Florida.
transportNotes: I-Drive location with large bus parking area. Group event packages available.
tips: Massive indoor space makes it a great rainy day option
Auto museum is impressive even for non-car enthusiasts

name: Pirate's Dinner Adventure
description: Pirate's Dinner Adventure is an interactive dinner theater experience featuring a full-sized pirate ship, acrobatics, sword fighting, and audience participation alongside a multi-course meal. The swashbuckling show is popular with families and groups.
transportNotes: Located near I-Drive. Bus drop-off with group seating coordination.
tips: Great group activity combining dinner and entertainment
Book group seating together for the best experience

name: Medieval Times Orlando
address: 4510 W Irlo Bronson Memorial Hwy, Kissimmee, FL 34746
description: Medieval Times Dinner and Tournament features an authentic medieval jousting tournament, sword fights, and horsemanship alongside a four-course feast eaten with your hands. Groups cheer for their assigned knight in an exciting arena show.
transportNotes: Located on US 192 in Kissimmee. Large bus parking lot with group check-in.
tips: Arrive early for the pre-show medieval village experience
Group packages include preferred seating and extras

name: Blue Man Group Orlando
description: Blue Man Group at Universal CityWalk delivers an electrifying show combining music, art, science, comedy, and technology in a unique multi-sensory experience. The three blue men create an unforgettable evening of entertainment for all ages.
transportNotes: Located at Universal CityWalk. Bus access through the Universal transportation hub.
tips: No theme park admission needed - CityWalk access only
Poncho seats (first few rows) mean you might get splashed

name: Cirque du Soleil - Drawn to Life
address: 1478 E Buena Vista Dr, Orlando, FL 32830
description: Drawn to Life by Cirque du Soleil at Disney Springs is a collaboration with Walt Disney Animation Studios featuring acrobatics, choreography, and Disney animation brought to life on stage. The show is exclusive to the Disney Springs location.
transportNotes: Located at Disney Springs with bus access at the Disney Springs bus loop. No park admission needed.
tips: No Disney park ticket needed - Disney Springs is free to enter
Book group tickets for best pricing and seating

name: Boggy Creek Airboat Adventures
description: Boggy Creek Airboat Adventures offers airboat tours through Central Florida's headwaters where guests can spot alligators, bald eagles, and other native wildlife. The tours range from 30-minute rides to hour-long nature tours through the Everglades ecosystem.
transportNotes: About 30 minutes south of Orlando. Bus parking at the dock staging area.
tips: Book private airboat tours for smaller groups
Great add-on to a Kissimmee area day trip

name: Wild Florida Airboats and Gator Park
description: Wild Florida offers airboat tours through 4,200 acres of protected wetlands plus a gator park with exotic animal encounters. The safari park features sloths, zebras, and other animals alongside native Florida wildlife.
transportNotes: About 45 minutes south of Orlando. Bus parking with group discounts available.
tips: Combo airboat and gator park tickets save money
Drive-through safari experience available

name: Give Kids the World Village
description: Give Kids the World Village is a 89-acre nonprofit resort in Kissimmee providing cost-free week-long vacations to critically ill children and their families. The whimsical village features themed villas, entertainment venues, and the iconic Castle of Miracles.
transportNotes: Located in Kissimmee near the US 192 corridor. Bus access for volunteer groups and special events.
tips: Volunteer group opportunities available throughout the year
A meaningful group outing that gives back to the community

name: Lake Eola Park
description: Lake Eola Park is the heart of downtown Orlando, featuring swan-shaped pedal boats, a Walt Disney Amphitheater, Orlando Farmers Market on Sundays, and the iconic Linton Allen Fountain. The park offers a scenic 0.9-mile walking path around the lake.
transportNotes: Downtown Orlando with bus drop-off on Robinson or Washington Streets. Metered parking nearby.
tips: Sunday Farmers Market is a great group outing
Swan boats are a fun team activity

name: Leu Gardens
description: Leu Gardens encompasses 50 acres of beautiful gardens including the largest formal rose garden in Florida, a tropical stream garden, butterfly garden, and ancient oaks draped in Spanish moss. The gardens host events, concerts, and movie nights throughout the year.
transportNotes: Located in central Orlando near Loch Haven Park. Bus parking at the main lot.
tips: Rose garden peaks in spring and fall
Outdoor movie nights are a fun group activity

name: Kraft Azalea Garden
description: Kraft Azalea Garden is a peaceful 5-acre park on the shore of Lake Maitland in Winter Park, featuring huge cypress trees, blooming azaleas, and a stone Exedra monument on the waterfront. The garden is a popular spot for small weddings and photography.
transportNotes: Small Winter Park garden. Bus drop-off on Alabama Drive. Better for smaller groups.
tips: Popular small wedding venue - transport the wedding party here
Azalea bloom peaks in March and April

name: Sunken Gardens
description: Sunken Gardens is one of the oldest living museums in the Tampa Bay area, featuring over 50,000 tropical plants and flowers in a lush garden setting that sits below street level. The century-old attraction hosts weddings, events, and educational programs.
transportNotes: Located on 4th Street in St. Petersburg. Bus parking on the street with group entrance.
tips: One of St. Pete's hidden gems
Popular wedding and event venue with group transport options

name: Florida Botanical Gardens
description: The Florida Botanical Gardens spans 182 acres of cultivated gardens, natural areas, and wetlands in Pinellas County. The gardens feature a wedding garden, tropical walk, butterfly garden, and a holiday lights display that draws huge crowds each December.
transportNotes: Located in Largo between Tampa and Clearwater. Free admission with bus parking available.
tips: Holiday lights event in December is a must-see
Free admission makes it a great budget group stop

name: Marie Selby Botanical Gardens
description: Marie Selby Botanical Gardens is a world-renowned botanical garden on Sarasota Bay specializing in orchids, bromeliads, and epiphytes. The 45-acre downtown campus and historic Spanish Point campus offer waterfront beauty and world-class horticulture.
transportNotes: Bayfront location in downtown Sarasota. Bus drop-off on Mound Street.
tips: Orchid collection is one of the finest in the world
Bayfront views make this a scenic group outing

name: Arthur R. Marshall Loxahatchee National Wildlife Refuge
description: Loxahatchee National Wildlife Refuge protects 143,954 acres of northern Everglades habitat, providing a home for alligators, wading birds, and the endangered snail kite. The refuge offers hiking, kayaking, and fishing opportunities.
transportNotes: About 30 minutes from West Palm Beach. Bus parking at the visitor center.
tips: Bring binoculars for bird watching
Cypress swamp boardwalk is accessible and scenic

name: Lion Country Safari
address: 2003 Lion Country Safari Rd, Loxahatchee, FL 33470
description: Lion Country Safari is a drive-through wildlife preserve where visitors ride through habitats with over 900 animals roaming free, including lions, giraffes, zebras, and rhinos. The adjacent Safari World amusement park adds rides and animal encounters.
transportNotes: About 30 minutes west of West Palm Beach. Charter buses can drive through the safari - a unique bus experience.
tips: Charter bus can drive the safari route - a unique group experience
Adjacent amusement park included with admission

name: Flamingo Gardens
description: Flamingo Gardens is a 60-acre botanical garden and wildlife sanctuary in Davie featuring Florida native wildlife, a tram ride through tropical and subtropical vegetation, and the historic Wray Home Museum. The facility houses non-releasable birds of prey, alligators, and flamingos.
transportNotes: Located in Davie, about 20 minutes from Fort Lauderdale. Bus parking available.
tips: Tram ride through the gardens is included with admission
Great for nature and wildlife groups visiting South Florida

name: Butterfly World
address: 3600 W Sample Rd, Coconut Creek, FL 33073
description: Butterfly World is the largest butterfly park in the world, featuring thousands of live butterflies from around the globe in lush tropical aviaries. The park also includes hummingbird and lorikeet encounters, insect exhibits, and botanical gardens.
transportNotes: Located in Tradewinds Park in Coconut Creek. Bus parking at the park entrance.
tips: The butterfly aviaries are best visited on sunny mornings
Lorikeet encounter is a fun group add-on

name: Morikami Museum and Japanese Gardens
address: 4000 Morikami Park Rd, Delray Beach, FL 33446
description: The Morikami Museum and Japanese Gardens is a unique cultural center celebrating the living connection between South Florida and Japan. The museum features rotating exhibitions and six distinct Japanese gardens spread across 200 acres of pine forest.
transportNotes: Located west of Delray Beach. Bus parking in the main lot with group entrance.
tips: The gardens alone are worth the visit
Monthly Japanese festival events draw big crowds

name: South Florida Science Center and Aquarium
address: 4801 Dreher Trail N, West Palm Beach, FL 33405
description: The South Florida Science Center features interactive exhibits, a planetarium, aquarium, miniature golf course, and a freshwater conservation area. The center offers hands-on STEM learning experiences for visitors of all ages.
transportNotes: Located near downtown West Palm Beach. Bus parking available in the main lot.
tips: Miniature golf and planetarium are popular group add-ons
Great for school STEM field trips

name: Ponce de Leon Inlet Lighthouse
address: 4931 S Peninsula Dr, Ponce Inlet, FL 32127
description: The Ponce de Leon Inlet Lighthouse is the tallest lighthouse in Florida at 175 feet and a National Historic Landmark. Visitors can climb all 203 steps to the top for panoramic views of the Atlantic Ocean, Mosquito Lagoon, and the surrounding coastline.
transportNotes: Located at Ponce Inlet near Daytona Beach. Bus parking in the lot off Peninsula Drive.
tips: 203 steps to the top - make sure your group is up for the climb
Museum buildings on the grounds are included with admission

name: Daytona Beach Boardwalk and Pier
address: 12 N Ocean Ave, Daytona Beach, FL 32118
description: The Daytona Beach Boardwalk features classic seaside amusement rides, arcades, and the Daytona Beach Pier extending into the Atlantic Ocean. The boardwalk area includes restaurants, shops, and entertainment along the world-famous beach.
transportNotes: Bus drop-off on Ocean Avenue. Walking distance to beachfront hotels and attractions.
tips: Classic boardwalk experience for evening group outings
Pier offers fishing and ocean views

name: Winter Park Scenic Boat Tour
address: 312 E Morse Blvd, Winter Park, FL 32789
description: The Winter Park Scenic Boat Tour is an hour-long narrated cruise through the chain of lakes and canals of Winter Park, passing by historic mansions, Rollins College, and lush tropical scenery. The tour has been operating since 1938.
transportNotes: Located at the east end of Morse Blvd in Winter Park. Bus drop-off with nearby parking.
tips: Book the full boat for private group tours
Combine with Park Avenue shopping and dining

name: Orlando Watersports Complex
description: Orlando Watersports Complex is a premier cable wakeboarding and waterskiing facility offering lessons and sessions for all skill levels. The cable system pulls riders around the lake, making it accessible for beginners while challenging for pros.
transportNotes: Located south of Orlando off Florida Rock Road. Bus parking with group packages.
tips: Great corporate team-building activity
No experience needed - lessons included for groups

name: iFLY Indoor Skydiving Orlando
description: iFLY Orlando offers indoor skydiving experiences in a vertical wind tunnel, simulating the thrill of freefall without jumping from a plane. Professional instructors guide flyers of all ages and skill levels through the experience.
transportNotes: I-Drive location with easy bus access. Group flight packages available.
tips: Group packages offer the best per-person rates
No experience needed - instructors guide the full experience

name: Bonnet Springs Park
description: Bonnet Springs Park is a 168-acre nature park and cultural venue in Lakeland featuring botanical gardens, nature trails, a children's museum, and event spaces. The park transformed a former industrial rail site into one of Central Florida's newest outdoor destinations.
transportNotes: Located in Lakeland between Tampa and Orlando. Bus parking at the main entrance.
tips: One of Central Florida's newest parks - modern facilities
Children's museum is great for family groups

name: Circle B Bar Reserve
description: Circle B Bar Reserve is a 1,267-acre nature preserve in Lakeland renowned for incredible bird watching and alligator sightings along Lake Hancock. The reserve features well-maintained trails through marshes, hardwood hammocks, and along the lakeshore.
transportNotes: About 30 minutes from Tampa or Orlando. Limited bus access but parking lot can accommodate larger vehicles.
tips: One of the best bird watching locations in Florida
Early morning visits offer the best wildlife sightings

name: Old Town Kissimmee
address: 5770 W Irlo Bronson Memorial Hwy, Kissimmee, FL 34746
description: Old Town is a vintage-themed entertainment and shopping district on US 192 in Kissimmee featuring classic car shows every Friday and Saturday night, amusement rides, restaurants, and unique shops. The district captures a 1950s-era small-town feel.
transportNotes: Located on US 192. Bus parking lot with easy group access.
tips: Friday and Saturday night car shows are a free group activity
Great evening option for groups staying on US 192

name: Pointe Orlando
description: Pointe Orlando is an open-air dining and entertainment complex on International Drive featuring restaurants, shops, a Regal cinema, B.B. King's Blues Club, and the Improv Comedy Club. The complex is a popular evening destination for groups.
transportNotes: Central I-Drive location across from the convention center. Bus drop-off at the main entrance.
tips: Great for convention attendee evening outings
Improv Comedy Club is a fun group activity

name: John's Pass Village and Boardwalk
description: John's Pass Village and Boardwalk is a charming waterfront village with over 100 shops, restaurants, and attractions on the Gulf of Mexico. The village offers dolphin cruises, jet ski rentals, fishing charters, and pirate ship tours.
transportNotes: Located on Gulf Boulevard in Madeira Beach. Bus parking available near the village entrance.
tips: Dolphin cruise from the village is a great group add-on
Fresh seafood restaurants right on the water

name: Tarpon Springs Sponge Docks
description: The Tarpon Springs Sponge Docks is a historic Greek-American community known for natural sponge diving, authentic Greek restaurants, and waterfront shops. The area has the highest percentage of Greek Americans in any U.S. city.
transportNotes: About 30 minutes north of Tampa/Clearwater. Bus parking along Dodecanese Boulevard.
tips: Greek food here is some of the best outside of Greece
Sponge diving boat tours tell the fascinating history

name: Weeki Wachee Springs State Park
description: Weeki Wachee Springs is a Florida icon famous for its live mermaid shows performed underwater since 1947. The park also features a water park, kayak and paddleboard rentals on the crystal-clear Weeki Wachee River, and a wildlife show.
transportNotes: About 60 minutes north of Tampa. Bus parking at the main entrance.
tips: Mermaid shows are a uniquely Florida experience
Kayak the river for a peaceful natural spring adventure

name: Florida Keys Day Trip (Key Largo)
description: Key Largo is the first island in the Florida Keys and the gateway to John Pennekamp Coral Reef State Park - the first undersea park in the United States. Glass-bottom boat tours, snorkeling, and diving in the coral reef are the main attractions.
transportNotes: About 90 minutes south of Fort Lauderdale. Charter bus brings groups to Key Largo for boat tours and snorkeling.
tips: Glass-bottom boat is a great option for non-swimmers
Book snorkel tours in advance for groups

name: McKee Botanical Garden
description: McKee Botanical Garden is an 18-acre tropical garden on the National Register of Historic Places, featuring a stunning collection of water lilies, orchids, and native Florida plants. The garden's historic Hall of Giants and lily pad-covered ponds are iconic landmarks.
transportNotes: Located on US 1 in Vero Beach. Bus parking at the entrance.
tips: Water lily collection is world-class
Spring and fall are the best seasons for blooms

name: IMAG History and Science Center
description: The IMAG History and Science Center in Fort Myers features interactive science and history exhibits, a living lab with touch tanks and aquariums, and a 3D theater. The museum covers Southwest Florida's history, ecology, and scientific discovery.
transportNotes: Located in downtown Fort Myers. Bus parking available on Cranford Avenue.
tips: Living lab with aquariums is popular with school groups
Good add-on when visiting Edison and Ford Estates

name: Naples Botanical Garden
description: Naples Botanical Garden is a 170-acre world-class garden featuring cultivated gardens representing tropical ecosystems from around the world, plus 90 acres of restored natural habitats. The garden is one of the newest and most stunning in Florida.
transportNotes: Located on Bayshore Drive in Naples. Bus parking at the main entrance.
tips: One of the newest world-class gardens in Florida
Allow 2-3 hours for the full garden experience

name: Imagine Museum
description: The Imagine Museum showcases one of the most comprehensive collections of American studio glass art in the country, featuring works by Dale Chihuly, Harvey Littleton, and other pioneer glass artists. The museum brings contemporary glass art to the forefront.
transportNotes: Located on Central Avenue in the St. Pete arts district. Bus drop-off with street parking.
tips: Part of the St. Pete arts district - combine with other galleries
Glass art collection is truly unique

name: The Baker Museum
description: The Baker Museum at Artis-Naples is Southwest Florida's premier visual arts museum, featuring 15 galleries of modern and contemporary art plus a glass dome conservatory with a Dale Chihuly installation. The museum is part of the Artis-Naples campus.
transportNotes: Located at the Artis-Naples campus. Bus parking in the main lot.
tips: Chihuly glass dome is a must-see
Combine with a performance at Artis-Naples

name: Bishop Museum of Science and Nature
description: The Bishop Museum of Science and Nature features a planetarium, aquarium, natural history exhibits, and a manatee rehabilitation center. The museum offers a comprehensive look at Florida's natural history from prehistoric times to the present.
transportNotes: Located in downtown Bradenton on the Manatee River. Bus parking on 10th Street.
tips: Manatee rehabilitation center is a highlight
Planetarium shows included with admission

name: Boca Raton Museum of Art
description: The Boca Raton Museum of Art features a significant collection of modern and contemporary art including photography, sculpture, and mixed media. Located in Mizner Park, the museum is the cultural anchor of downtown Boca Raton.
transportNotes: Located in Mizner Park in downtown Boca Raton. Bus drop-off on Plaza Real.
tips: Mizner Park has excellent dining options for groups
Free admission on certain days

name: Perez Art Museum Miami
description: Perez Art Museum Miami (PAMM) is a contemporary art museum overlooking Biscayne Bay, featuring hanging gardens, waterfront terraces, and a collection focused on 20th and 21st century art from the Americas, Western Europe, and Africa.
transportNotes: Located in Museum Park in downtown Miami. Bus drop-off on Biscayne Boulevard.
tips: Waterfront terrace offers stunning bay views
Near Frost Science Museum for a museum double visit

name: Phillip and Patricia Frost Museum of Science
description: The Frost Museum of Science is a state-of-the-art science museum on Biscayne Bay featuring a planetarium, aquarium with a 500,000-gallon Gulf Stream tank, and interactive exhibits covering biology, physics, and the Everglades ecosystem.
transportNotes: Located next to PAMM in Museum Park. Same bus drop-off area on Biscayne Boulevard.
tips: The aquarium cone tank is architecturally stunning
Combo visit with PAMM next door for a full museum day

name: Jungle Island
description: Jungle Island is an interactive zoological park on Watson Island in Miami featuring exotic animals, a Neotropical exhibit, zip line, and the SuperFlight wind tunnel experience. The park blends wildlife encounters with adventure activities.
transportNotes: Located on Watson Island via MacArthur Causeway. Bus parking at the main entrance.
tips: Zip line and wind tunnel add adventure to the wildlife visit
Great corporate event venue options

name: Zoo Miami
description: Zoo Miami is the largest and oldest zoo in Florida, featuring over 3,000 animals on 750 acres in a cageless environment separated from visitors by moats. It is the only subtropical zoo in the continental United States.
transportNotes: Located in south Miami-Dade County. Bus parking at the main entrance with group rates.
tips: Cageless design makes for unique animal viewing
Tram ride available to cover the large grounds

name: Miami Seaquarium
description: Miami Seaquarium is a 38-acre oceanarium on Virginia Key featuring marine mammal shows, tropical fish exhibits, a sea turtle rehabilitation center, and a coral reef aquarium. The park has been a Miami landmark since 1955.
transportNotes: Located on Virginia Key via the Rickenbacker Causeway. Bus parking at the main entrance.
tips: Sea turtle rehabilitation center is an educational highlight
Beautiful Key Biscayne views from the park

name: Vizcaya Museum and Gardens
description: Vizcaya Museum and Gardens is a National Historic Landmark featuring a stunning Italian Renaissance-style villa built in 1916, surrounded by 10 acres of formal gardens on Biscayne Bay. The estate showcases European decorative arts and architecture.
transportNotes: Located on South Miami Avenue. Bus drop-off at the main entrance gate.
tips: One of Miami's most photographed locations
Popular wedding venue - transport wedding parties here

name: Wynwood Walls
description: Wynwood Walls is an outdoor street art museum in Miami's Wynwood Arts District featuring large-scale murals by world-renowned artists. The surrounding neighborhood has become one of the most vibrant art districts in the world with galleries, shops, and restaurants.
transportNotes: Located in the Wynwood Arts District. Bus drop-off on NW 2nd Avenue with nearby lot parking.
tips: Walk the surrounding blocks for additional street art
Art walks and gallery nights on the second Saturday of each month

name: Lake Louisa State Park
description: Lake Louisa State Park encompasses 4,372 acres with a chain of lakes, swimming beach, horseback riding trails, and some of the best views of the rolling hills of Central Florida. The park is a peaceful nature escape just 30 minutes from Disney.
transportNotes: Located west of Orlando near Clermont. Bus parking at the main beach and trailhead areas.
tips: Horseback riding available with advance booking
Scenic lake views are some of the best in Central Florida

name: Victoria and Albert's
description: Victoria and Albert's at Disney's Grand Floridian Resort is Central Florida's only AAA Five Diamond restaurant. The prix-fixe multi-course dining experience features seasonal ingredients, an extensive wine cellar, and impeccable service. Dinner is a 3-hour experience with a dedicated chef for each table.
transportNotes: Located inside Disney's Grand Floridian Resort. Accessible via monorail from Magic Kingdom or by car with resort parking.
tips: Reservations open 60 days in advance and sell out immediately
Dress code is strictly enforced - jackets required for men

name: California Grill
description: California Grill sits atop Disney's Contemporary Resort on the 15th floor, offering panoramic views of Magic Kingdom and its fireworks. The restaurant serves contemporary American cuisine with Pacific Rim influences, featuring sushi, seasonal plates, and an award-winning wine list.
transportNotes: Located on the 15th floor of Disney's Contemporary Resort. Accessible via monorail or by car.
tips: Return with your dinner receipt to watch the fireworks from the observation deck
Brunch on Sundays is outstanding and slightly easier to reserve

name: Bern's Steak House
description: Bern's Steak House is one of the most legendary steakhouses in America, operating since 1956. The restaurant ages its own beef, grows its own organic produce, and houses one of the largest wine collections in the world with over 500,000 bottles. The upstairs Harry Waugh Dessert Room is an experience unto itself.
transportNotes: Located in South Tampa's SoHo district. Street parking and valet available.
tips: The Harry Waugh Dessert Room upstairs is a must - order the macadamia nut ice cream pie
Kitchen tours are available and show the legendary wine cellar

name: Columbia Restaurant
description: The Columbia Restaurant in Ybor City is Florida's oldest restaurant, founded in 1905. The sprawling 1,700-seat restaurant occupies an entire city block and serves traditional Spanish and Cuban cuisine. The 1905 Salad prepared tableside is iconic, and the flamenco dancing performances add to the authentic atmosphere.
transportNotes: Located in the heart of Ybor City. TECO streetcar stop nearby. Free parking in adjacent garage.
tips: The 1905 Salad prepared tableside is a must-order signature dish
Flamenco dancing shows happen nightly on select nights

name: The Ravenous Pig
address: 565 W Fairbanks Ave, Winter Park, FL 32789
description: The Ravenous Pig is a James Beard-nominated gastropub in Winter Park serving creative American cuisine with Southern influences. The restaurant also operates its own brewery on-site, Cask and Larder, producing craft beers that pair with the seasonal menu.
transportNotes: Located in Winter Park near Park Avenue. Free parking in adjacent lot.
tips: The Pig Wings (crispy pork shank) are the signature dish
The on-site brewery serves excellent house-brewed beers

name: Christini's Ristorante Italiano
description: Christini's is Orlando's premier fine Italian restaurant, serving Northern Italian cuisine in an elegant setting on Restaurant Row. Tableside preparations, fresh pasta, and an extensive Italian wine list have earned the restaurant accolades for over 30 years.
transportNotes: Located on Restaurant Row in Dr. Phillips. Valet parking available.
tips: Tableside Caesar salad and flambe desserts are theatrical and delicious
Reservations strongly recommended for weekends

name: Le Cellier Steakhouse
address: 1510 N Avenue of the Stars, Orlando, FL 32830
description: Le Cellier Steakhouse in EPCOT's Canada pavilion serves premium steaks, Canadian cheddar cheese soup, and pretzel bread in a cozy wine cellar atmosphere. It is one of the most sought-after dining reservations in all of Walt Disney World.
transportNotes: Located inside EPCOT's Canada pavilion in World Showcase. Theme park admission required.
tips: Book exactly 60 days out at 6 AM for the best chance at a reservation
The Canadian cheddar cheese soup and pretzel bread are legendary

name: The BOATHOUSE
address: 1620 E Buena Vista Dr, Orlando, FL 32830
description: The BOATHOUSE at Disney Springs is an upscale waterfront restaurant serving steaks, chops, and fresh seafood alongside vintage boat tours on the lake. The restaurant also features an impressive collection of dream boats and classic amphibious cars that take guests on tours of the lagoon.
transportNotes: Located at Disney Springs, The Landing. Free parking in Disney Springs garages.
tips: Amphicar tours take you for a ride in a vintage 1960s car that drives into the water
The raw bar and lobster bake are excellent

name: Ohana at Disney's Polynesian Village
description: Ohana at Disney's Polynesian Village Resort serves an all-you-can-eat family-style dinner featuring sweet bread, mixed greens, peel-and-eat shrimp, grilled chicken and steak skewers cooked over a fire pit, noodles, and the legendary Ohana bread pudding dessert.
transportNotes: Located at Disney's Polynesian Village Resort. Monorail access from Magic Kingdom.
tips: The bread pudding with caramel sauce is legendary - leave room for it
Request a window table for views of the Magic Kingdom fireworks

name: Ulele
