description: Ulele is a Tampa Riverwalk restaurant set in a beautifully restored 1903 water pump building. The restaurant celebrates Florida's Native American heritage with a menu featuring wood-fired and charcoal-grilled local ingredients, an on-site brewery, and stunning riverfront views.
transportNotes: Located on the Tampa Riverwalk in Tampa Heights. Free parking in lot.
tips: The fire-roasted oysters and charcoal-grilled shrimp are standouts
Sit on the patio for beautiful Hillsborough River views

name: Rusty Pelican
address: 2425 N Rocky Point Dr, Tampa, FL 33607
description: The Rusty Pelican sits on Rocky Point overlooking Tampa Bay, offering panoramic water views and fresh seafood. The restaurant has been a Tampa Bay dining institution since 1978 and features a raw bar, fresh catches, and one of the best sunset views in the area.
transportNotes: Located on Rocky Point, near the Courtney Campbell Causeway. Free parking.
tips: Request a window or patio table for sunset views over Tampa Bay
The Sunday brunch is one of the best in Tampa

name: Rocco's Tacos and Tequila Bar
address: 7468 W Sand Lake Rd, Orlando, FL 32819
description: Rocco's Tacos on Restaurant Row is known for its tableside guacamole, sizzling fajitas, and an extensive tequila selection with over 300 varieties. The lively atmosphere and generous portions make it a popular spot on Orlando's Restaurant Row.
transportNotes: Located on Restaurant Row in Dr. Phillips. Free parking in lot.
tips: The tableside guacamole is a must-order experience
Happy hour features excellent drink and food specials

name: Seasons 52
address: 7700 W Sand Lake Rd, Orlando, FL 32819
description: Seasons 52 is an upscale restaurant on Restaurant Row known for its seasonally inspired menu where no item exceeds 475 calories. The restaurant features an extensive wine list with 100+ selections by the glass, fresh grilled items, and their famous Mini Indulgence desserts.
transportNotes: Located on Restaurant Row in Dr. Phillips. Free parking in lot.
tips: The flatbreads and wood-fire grilled items are excellent
Mini Indulgence desserts are the perfect ending - try the key lime pie

name: Capa Steakhouse at Four Seasons
description: Capa is a rooftop steakhouse atop the Four Seasons Resort Orlando, offering panoramic views of Disney's fireworks from both Magic Kingdom and EPCOT. The Spanish-inspired menu features premium steaks, wood-grilled seafood, and an award-winning cocktail program.
transportNotes: Located at the Four Seasons Resort Orlando near Disney. Valet parking available.
tips: Request a terrace table to watch Disney fireworks from both Magic Kingdom and EPCOT
The wagyu beef tartare and grilled octopus are exceptional starters

name: Highball and Harvest
description: Highball and Harvest at the Ritz-Carlton Orlando, Grande Lakes serves Southern-inspired farm-to-table cuisine using ingredients from the hotel's own citrus grove and rooftop garden. The restaurant features craft cocktails, a bourbon program, and warm Southern hospitality.
transportNotes: Located at the Ritz-Carlton Orlando, Grande Lakes. Valet parking available.
tips: The biscuits with house-made jam are a must
The bourbon flight is an excellent way to sample the collection

name: Cafe Tu Tu Tango
description: Cafe Tu Tu Tango on International Drive is themed as an artist's loft with live artists painting on easels throughout the restaurant. The menu features small tapas-style plates from multiple cuisines, encouraging sharing and sampling a wide variety of flavors.
transportNotes: Located on International Drive. Free parking in lot.
tips: The all-tapas menu is designed for sharing - order several plates for the table
Live artists paint throughout the restaurant and sell their work

name: Ocean Prime Orlando
address: 7339 W Sand Lake Rd, Orlando, FL 32819
description: Ocean Prime on Restaurant Row serves prime steaks and fresh seafood in an upscale, modern atmosphere. The restaurant is known for its signature smoking cocktails, excellent raw bar, and attentive service.
transportNotes: Located on Restaurant Row in Dr. Phillips. Valet parking available.
tips: The Berries and Bubbles cocktail (served smoking with dry ice) is the signature drink
The 10 oz prime filet and Colorado lamb chops are outstanding

name: Kabooki Sushi
description: Kabooki Sushi is a James Beard-nominated omakase sushi experience in Orlando's Mills 50 district. Chef Henry Moso serves innovative Japanese cuisine combining traditional techniques with Florida ingredients. The intimate space offers both omakase counter seating and a la carte dining.
transportNotes: Located in Orlando's Mills 50 Vietnamese district. Street parking available.
tips: The omakase counter experience is worth the splurge
Located in the Mills 50 district - combine with Vietnamese food exploration

name: Docking Bay 7 Food and Cargo
description: Docking Bay 7 Food and Cargo is the main quick-service restaurant in Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge at Hollywood Studios. The food is uniquely themed to the Star Wars universe with dishes like Smoked Kaadu Ribs, Fried Endorian Tip-Yip, and Blue Milk. The industrial spaceport theming is immersive.
transportNotes: Located inside Disney's Hollywood Studios in Galaxy's Edge. Theme park admission required.
tips: The Smoked Kaadu Ribs are the best dish on the menu
Try the blue or green milk at the Milk Stand nearby

name: Park Avenue Winter Park
description: Park Avenue is Winter Park's charming main street, lined with boutique shops, art galleries, sidewalk cafes, and upscale restaurants beneath a canopy of live oak trees. The street stretches from Rollins College to the Morse Museum, offering a sophisticated shopping and dining experience.
transportNotes: Located in downtown Winter Park. SunRail station nearby. Metered street parking and parking garage.
tips: The Farmers Market on Saturday mornings is a local favorite
Walk to the nearby Morse Museum for the world's largest Tiffany glass collection

name: Orlando International Premium Outlets
description: Orlando International Premium Outlets is an open-air outlet mall on International Drive featuring over 180 designer and brand-name stores including Nike, Adidas, Coach, Michael Kors, and Polo Ralph Lauren at discount prices.
transportNotes: Located on International Drive. I-Ride Trolley stop at the entrance. Large free parking lot.
tips: Download the VIP Coupon Book from the Simon app for additional discounts
This is the more popular of the two Premium Outlets locations

name: The Mall at Millenia
description: The Mall at Millenia is Orlando's most upscale shopping destination, featuring luxury brands like Louis Vuitton, Gucci, Tiffany and Co., Chanel, and Burberry alongside anchors Bloomingdale's, Macy's, and Neiman Marcus.
transportNotes: Located off I-4 near the Convention Center area. Large free parking garage.
tips: The food court and restaurants are more upscale than typical malls
The Cheesecake Factory and Capital Grille are on-site for dining

name: Las Olas Boulevard Shopping District
address: Las Olas Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33301
description: Las Olas Boulevard is Fort Lauderdale's premier shopping, dining, and entertainment street, stretching from the Riverwalk to the beach. The tree-lined boulevard features boutique shops, art galleries, over 60 restaurants and bars, and a vibrant nightlife scene.
transportNotes: Located in downtown Fort Lauderdale. Water Taxi access. Metered street parking and parking garages.
tips: The Water Taxi stops along Las Olas and provides a fun way to explore
Gallery walks and wine events happen on the first Friday of each month

name: Worth Avenue
description: Worth Avenue is one of the most exclusive shopping streets in the world, running from the Intracoastal Waterway to the Atlantic Ocean on Palm Beach island. The Mediterranean-style architecture houses luxury brands including Chanel, Hermes, Cartier, and Ralph Lauren in an elegant setting.
transportNotes: Located on Palm Beach island. Metered street parking. Access via Royal Park Bridge from West Palm Beach.
tips: The hidden courtyards (vias) branching off Worth Avenue are charming and worth exploring
Window shopping is free and the architecture alone is worth the visit

name: St. Armands Circle
description: St. Armands Circle is a circular shopping and dining district on St. Armands Key in Sarasota, developed by John Ringling in the 1920s. The area features upscale boutiques, galleries, and restaurants surrounding a central park with statues.
transportNotes: Located on St. Armands Key between Sarasota and Lido Beach. Free parking throughout.
tips: Combine shopping with a trip to nearby Lido Beach
Columbia Restaurant here is an outpost of the famous Ybor City original

name: Pointe Orlando
description: Pointe Orlando is an open-air entertainment and shopping complex on International Drive featuring restaurants, specialty shops, a Regal cinema, and the Improv Comedy Club. The complex is a popular evening destination for I-Drive visitors.
transportNotes: Located on International Drive near the Convention Center. I-Ride Trolley stop. Free parking garage.
tips: The Improv Comedy Club hosts well-known comedians regularly
BB King's Blues Club offers live music and Southern food nightly

name: Old Town Kissimmee
address: 5770 W Irlo Bronson Hwy, Kissimmee, FL 34746
description: Old Town Kissimmee is a walking district of shops, restaurants, and amusements on US-192. The area features a weekly Saturday night classic car cruise, amusement rides, haunted houses, and over 70 specialty shops in a charming boardwalk setting.
transportNotes: Located on US-192 near Fun Spot Kissimmee. Free parking in lot.
tips: The Saturday night classic car cruise draws hundreds of vintage vehicles
Located next to Fun Spot Kissimmee for combined entertainment

name: Sawgrass Mills
description: Sawgrass Mills is the largest outlet and value retail shopping destination in South Florida, with over 350 stores including The Colonnade Outlets featuring luxury brands like Burberry, Prada, Salvatore Ferragamo, and Tory Burch at outlet prices.
transportNotes: Located off Sawgrass Expressway in Sunrise. Massive free parking lot.
tips: The Colonnade Outlets section has the luxury brands at the best discounts
Budget an entire day - the mall is enormous with over 350 stores

name: International Plaza and Bay Street
description: International Plaza is Tampa's premier upscale shopping mall, anchored by Nordstrom and Neiman Marcus. Bay Street, the adjacent open-air dining and shopping district, features restaurants including The Capital Grille, The Cheesecake Factory, and Blue Martini.
transportNotes: Located near Tampa International Airport off Westshore Boulevard. Large free parking garages.
tips: Bay Street's restaurants and bars come alive in the evening
Combine with Westshore Plaza across the street for a full shopping day

name: Rosemary Square
address: 700 S Rosemary Ave, West Palm Beach, FL 33401
description: Rosemary Square (formerly CityPlace) is West Palm Beach's vibrant downtown shopping, dining, and entertainment destination. The Mediterranean-style complex features retailers, over 25 restaurants and bars, a Cinepolis Luxury Cinema, and regular live events.
transportNotes: Located in downtown West Palm Beach. Brightline train station within walking distance. Parking garages available.
tips: The Brightline train station is walking distance for quick access from Fort Lauderdale or Miami
The Thursday night green market is a popular local event

name: Mizner Park
description: Mizner Park is an upscale outdoor lifestyle center in downtown Boca Raton featuring shops, restaurants, the Boca Raton Museum of Art, and an outdoor amphitheater for concerts and events. The Mediterranean Revival architecture reflects Boca Raton's signature style.
transportNotes: Located in downtown Boca Raton. Free parking garage.
tips: The Boca Raton Museum of Art in Mizner Park is worth a visit
Friday Night Live events feature live music and an energetic atmosphere

name: Mennello Museum of American Art
description: The Mennello Museum of American Art is a small but exceptional museum on the shores of Lake Formosa in Orlando. The permanent collection features the folk art paintings of Earl Cunningham, and the museum hosts rotating exhibitions of American art.
transportNotes: Located in Orlando's Loch Haven Park cultural district near the Orlando Science Center. Free parking.
tips: The museum is in Loch Haven Park - combine with the Orlando Science Center and Orlando Museum of Art nearby
The lakeside sculpture garden is free to visit

name: Cornell Fine Arts Museum
description: The Cornell Fine Arts Museum at Rollins College is one of the oldest and most distinguished collections in Florida. The museum's 5,000+ works span from antiquity to contemporary art, including paintings, sculptures, and decorative arts. The lakeside campus location is beautiful.
transportNotes: Located on the Rollins College campus in Winter Park. Visitor parking available.
tips: Free admission - one of the best free cultural experiences in the Orlando area
Walk the beautiful Rollins College campus along Lake Virginia afterward

name: Charles Hosmer Morse Museum of American Art
address: 445 N Park Ave, Winter Park, FL 32789
description: The Morse Museum houses the world's most comprehensive collection of works by Louis Comfort Tiffany, including the spectacular Tiffany Chapel from the 1893 World's Columbian Exposition. The collection includes Tiffany's stained glass windows, lamps, pottery, and jewelry.
transportNotes: Located on Park Avenue in Winter Park. Metered street parking and parking garage nearby.
tips: Free admission on Friday evenings from 4-8 PM during November through April
The reconstructed Tiffany Chapel is the museum's centerpiece and is breathtaking

name: Zora Neale Hurston National Museum of Fine Arts
description: The Zora Neale Hurston Museum in Eatonville celebrates the legacy of the renowned author and anthropologist who grew up in America's oldest incorporated African American municipality. The museum features rotating art exhibitions and cultural programming.
transportNotes: Located in Eatonville, just north of Orlando. Free parking.
tips: The annual Zora! Festival in January/February is a major cultural event
Eatonville is a historically significant community worth exploring

name: Ponce de Leon Inlet Lighthouse and Museum
address: 4931 S Peninsula Dr, Ponce Inlet, FL 32127
description: The Ponce de Leon Inlet Lighthouse is the tallest lighthouse in Florida at 175 feet and a National Historic Landmark. Visitors can climb all 203 steps to the top for panoramic views of the inlet, ocean, and surrounding area. The museum complex includes the keeper's cottages with historical exhibits.
transportNotes: Located at the southern tip of the Daytona Beach peninsula in Ponce Inlet. Free parking.
tips: The climb to the top is 203 steps - worth it for the panoramic views
Visit at sunset for spectacular views from the top

name: Orlando Museum of Art
description: The Orlando Museum of Art in Loch Haven Park features a permanent collection of American art from the 18th century to the present, African art, and contemporary works. The museum hosts the annual 1st Thursdays events combining art, music, and food each month.
transportNotes: Located in Loch Haven Park cultural district. Free parking in lot.
tips: 1st Thursdays events are a popular social gathering with art, music, and drinks
Combine with the Mennello Museum and Science Center in the same park

name: Henry B. Plant Museum
description: The Henry B. Plant Museum is housed in the south wing of the historic Tampa Bay Hotel (now the University of Tampa), a stunning 1891 Moorish Revival masterpiece. The museum tells the story of Henry Plant's railroad empire and the lavish Tampa Bay Hotel that helped establish Tampa as a destination city.
transportNotes: Located on the University of Tampa campus. Visitor parking available along the Riverwalk.
tips: The building's Moorish Revival architecture with silver minarets is spectacular from the outside alone
Free admission on the first Thursday of each month

name: Florida Holocaust Museum
description: The Florida Holocaust Museum is one of the largest Holocaust museums in the United States. The museum houses a permanent collection of Holocaust artifacts including a boxcar used to transport prisoners, historical photographs, and survivor testimonies.
transportNotes: Located in downtown St. Petersburg near the waterfront. Metered street parking.
tips: Allow 2-3 hours for a meaningful visit through the exhibits
The museum is deeply moving - appropriate for older children and adults

name: Appleton Museum of Art
address: 4333 E Silver Springs Blvd, Ocala, FL 34470
description: The Appleton Museum of Art in Ocala houses an impressive collection of European, American, Asian, African, and contemporary art in a stunning Italian marble building. The museum was founded by Arthur I. Appleton and includes over 24,000 objects.
transportNotes: Located on Silver Springs Boulevard in Ocala. Free parking.
tips: The building itself is beautiful - made of Italian travertine marble
Combine with a visit to nearby Silver Springs State Park

name: Museum of Fine Arts St. Petersburg
address: 255 Beach Dr NE, St. Petersburg, FL 33701
description: The Museum of Fine Arts (MFA) on St. Petersburg's Beach Drive houses a world-class collection spanning 5,000 years of art history, including works by Monet, Rodin, O'Keeffe, and Renoir. The waterfront setting and sculpture garden make the visit especially pleasant.
transportNotes: Located on Beach Drive in downtown St. Petersburg. Metered parking and nearby garages.
tips: The waterfront sculpture garden is free to visit and beautiful
Beach Drive in St. Pete has excellent restaurants for before or after your visit

name: Imagine Museum
description: The Imagine Museum showcases one of the largest collections of contemporary studio glass art in the world. The museum features works by Dale Chihuly, Lino Tagliapietra, and other master glass artists in a beautifully lit gallery space.
transportNotes: Located on Central Avenue in St. Petersburg. Free parking in lot.
tips: Glass art photography is encouraged and the lighting is perfect for it
St. Petersburg's Grand Central Arts District surrounds the museum

name: Orange County Regional History Center
description: The Orange County Regional History Center is housed in the restored 1927 Orange County Courthouse in downtown Orlando. The museum tells the story of Central Florida from prehistoric times through the citrus industry, cattle ranching, and the arrival of the space program and theme parks.
transportNotes: Located in downtown Orlando near Lake Eola. Parking garages nearby.
tips: The exhibit on how theme parks transformed Central Florida is fascinating
Located in the beautifully restored 1927 courthouse building

name: Holocaust Memorial Resource and Education Center
description: The Holocaust Memorial Resource and Education Center in Maitland features a permanent exhibit on the Holocaust with artifacts, photographs, and survivor testimonials. The center serves as an educational resource for understanding the consequences of hatred and prejudice.
transportNotes: Located in Maitland, 15 minutes north of downtown Orlando. Free parking.
tips: The center offers powerful educational programming for school groups
Located near the Maitland Art Center, another cultural gem

name: West Orange Trail
address: 501 Crown Point Cross Rd, Winter Garden, FL 34787
description: The West Orange Trail is a 22-mile paved multi-use trail running from Apopka to Oakland through historic downtown Winter Garden. The trail passes through rolling citrus groves, lakeside scenery, and connects to the charming downtown Winter Garden with its shops, restaurants, and Garden Theatre.
transportNotes: Multiple trailheads with parking. The Winter Garden trailhead at the Killarney Station is the most popular starting point.
tips: The Winter Garden trailhead is the best starting point with bike rentals and shops nearby
Downtown Winter Garden along the trail has excellent restaurants and the Garden Theatre

name: Cross Seminole Trail
description: The Cross Seminole Trail is a 23-mile paved trail system connecting Oviedo, Winter Springs, and Lake Mary in Seminole County. The trail winds through oak hammocks, past lakes, and along the Little Big Econ State Forest.
transportNotes: Multiple trailheads with parking throughout Seminole County.
tips: The Oviedo trailhead near Black Hammock Adventures is a great starting point
The trail connects to the University of Central Florida campus area

name: Little Econ Greenway
description: The Little Econ Greenway is an 8-mile paved trail following the Little Econlockhatchee River through east Orlando. The trail connects Blanchard Park to Jay Blanchard Trail, passing through natural Florida hammock and wetland scenery.
transportNotes: Trailhead at Blanchard Park with free parking.
tips: Blanchard Park trailhead has restrooms, water fountains, and picnic areas
Watch for wildlife along the river sections including herons and turtles

name: Turkey Lake Park
description: Turkey Lake Park is a 300-acre city park near International Drive offering swimming, fishing, hiking, camping, and an ecology center. The park feels like a natural oasis just minutes from the tourist corridor and features a swimming pool, nature trails, and picnic pavilions.
transportNotes: Located off Hiawassee Road near I-Drive. Park entrance fee $5 per vehicle.
tips: One of the best-kept secrets near I-Drive - a natural escape minutes from the tourist corridor
The park has a swimming pool, fishing lake, and campground

name: Harry P. Leu Gardens
description: Harry P. Leu Gardens is a 50-acre botanical oasis in the heart of Orlando featuring the largest camellia collection outside of California, a tropical stream garden, butterfly garden, and the historic Leu House Museum. The rose garden blooms from October through May.
transportNotes: Located in Orlando's Audubon Park neighborhood near downtown. Free parking.
tips: The camellia and rose garden peak seasons are October through March
Movie nights and live music events are held on the grounds seasonally

name: Dickson Azalea Park
description: Dickson Azalea Park is a hidden gem in downtown Orlando's Thornton Park neighborhood. The park follows Fern Creek through a lush canopy of live oaks and azalea bushes, creating a shaded, peaceful walkway that feels like a different world from the surrounding city.
transportNotes: Located in Orlando's Thornton Park neighborhood near Lake Eola. Street parking.
tips: The azaleas bloom most brilliantly in February and March
Connects to Washington Street and the vibrant Thornton Park dining scene

name: Tibet-Butler Nature Preserve
description: Tibet-Butler Nature Preserve is a 440-acre natural area just minutes from Walt Disney World, featuring four hiking trails through pine flatwoods, scrub, and hardwood hammock. The preserve is home to gopher tortoises, white-tailed deer, and hundreds of bird species.
transportNotes: Located on CR-535 near Disney. Small parking lot at the nature center.
tips: A peaceful nature escape minutes from Disney - most tourists have no idea it exists
The Osprey Overlook trail offers lake views and is the most scenic trail

name: Wekiva Island
description: Wekiva Island is a private nature park on the Wekiva River offering kayak and paddleboard rentals, swimming in the spring-fed river, and a laid-back outdoor bar and grill. The clear, cool water and natural surroundings make it a popular escape for Orlando locals.
transportNotes: Located in Longwood, about 30 minutes north of downtown Orlando. Limited parking - arrive early on weekends.
tips: Arrive early on weekends as parking fills up quickly
The outdoor bar serves food and drinks right on the riverbank

name: Shingle Creek Regional Trail
description: The Shingle Creek Regional Trail follows the headwaters of the Florida Everglades through a scenic creek corridor. The paved trail stretches from Kissimmee to the convention center area and passes through beautiful wetland and creek scenery.
transportNotes: Multiple trailheads with parking. The Steffee Homestead trailhead in Kissimmee is a popular starting point.
tips: Shingle Creek is technically the headwaters of the Everglades
The trail passes through beautiful wetland areas with abundant wildlife

name: Oakland Nature Preserve
description: Oakland Nature Preserve is a 128-acre natural area on the shores of Lake Apopka featuring boardwalk trails through cypress swamps, educational exhibits, and outstanding birdwatching. The preserve connects to the West Orange Trail.
transportNotes: Located in Oakland near Winter Garden. Free parking.
tips: The boardwalk through the cypress swamp is stunning for photography
Connects to the West Orange Trail for extended hiking or biking

name: Green Springs Park
description: Green Springs Park near DeLand is a unique geological site featuring a sulfur spring that creates a vivid green pool surrounded by tropical vegetation. The small park has a boardwalk trail circling the spring and surrounding wetlands.
transportNotes: Located in Enterprise near DeLand. Small parking lot at entrance.
tips: The vivid green color of the spring water is caused by sulfur algae and is photogenic
A small, lesser-known gem near DeLand and Blue Spring State Park

name: Topgolf Orlando
description: Topgolf Orlando is a multi-level driving range and entertainment venue near International Drive. Each bay features climate-controlled hitting areas with microchipped balls that track distance and accuracy. The venue includes a full restaurant, bars, and event spaces.
transportNotes: Located on Universal Boulevard near I-Drive. Large free parking lot.
tips: No golf experience needed - the games are designed for all skill levels
Weekend evenings are the busiest - weekday afternoons offer walk-in availability

name: Dave and Buster's Orlando
description: Dave and Buster's on International Drive is a large entertainment complex combining a full restaurant and bar with hundreds of arcade games, virtual reality experiences, and sports viewing on massive screens.
transportNotes: Located on International Drive. I-Ride Trolley access. Free parking.
tips: Half-price games on Wednesdays make it the best value day
The Power Card unlimited play option is best for longer visits

name: Main Event Entertainment
address: 8050 Red Bug Lake Rd, Oviedo, FL 32765
description: Main Event Entertainment is a family entertainment center featuring bowling, laser tag, gravity ropes, billiards, arcade games, and a full restaurant and bar. The multi-activity venue is ideal for groups and family outings.
transportNotes: Located in Oviedo, east Orlando. Free parking.
tips: The gravity ropes course is thrilling and suitable for all ages
Unlimited play packages offer the best value for extended visits

name: iFLY Indoor Skydiving Orlando
description: iFLY Orlando offers indoor skydiving in a vertical wind tunnel that simulates freefall at up to 175 mph. Professional instructors guide first-timers through the experience. Located on International Drive, the facility offers flight packages for ages 3 and up.
transportNotes: Located on International Drive. I-Ride Trolley access. Free parking.
tips: No experience needed - the instructor guides you the entire time
The high-fly experience (going higher in the tunnel) is worth the upgrade

name: Madame Tussauds Orlando
description: Madame Tussauds Orlando at ICON Park features incredibly lifelike wax figures of celebrities, athletes, musicians, historical figures, and movie characters in interactive themed zones designed for photos and social media content.
transportNotes: Located at ICON Park on International Drive. Free parking in ICON Park garage.
tips: Combo tickets with The Wheel and SEA LIFE offer significant savings
The Marvel and Star Wars figures are the most popular photo spots

name: Escapology Escape Rooms Orlando
description: Escapology is a premium escape room experience on International Drive featuring multiple themed rooms with varying difficulty levels. Teams of 2-8 players work together to solve puzzles, find clues, and escape within 60 minutes.
transportNotes: Located on International Drive. Free parking in plaza lot.
tips: Book in advance as popular time slots sell out
Groups of 4-6 tend to work best for most rooms

name: K1 Speed Orlando
description: K1 Speed Orlando is an indoor electric go-kart racing facility featuring high-performance karts on a professionally designed track. The facility uses arrival-and-drive format with electronic timing for competitive racing.
transportNotes: Located on Semoran Boulevard near Orlando International Airport. Free parking.
tips: The karts are fast electric models - more intense than tourist go-karts
Group events and corporate packages are available with private track time

name: Crayola Experience Orlando
address: 8001 S Orange Blossom Trail, Orlando, FL 32809
description: Crayola Experience Orlando at The Florida Mall is a 70,000-square-foot family attraction featuring 26 hands-on activities where kids (and adults) can create art, name and wrap their own crayons, star in coloring pages, and explore color science.
transportNotes: Located inside The Florida Mall. Free mall parking.
tips: Naming and wrapping your own crayon is a fun souvenir to take home
Best for children ages 3-12 who love art and creative play

name: Dezerland Park Orlando
description: Dezerland Park is a massive 800,000-square-foot indoor entertainment and automotive museum on International Drive. The facility houses over 2,000 classic and exotic cars, go-kart racing, trampoline parks, bowling, axe throwing, and an extensive arcade.
transportNotes: Located on International Drive. Free parking in lot.
tips: The car collection alone is worth the visit for automotive enthusiasts
The facility is enormous - plan several hours for all activities

name: SEA LIFE Orlando Aquarium
description: SEA LIFE Orlando Aquarium at ICON Park features a 360-degree ocean tunnel, touch pools, and thousands of marine creatures in themed habitats. The aquarium offers a compact but engaging marine life experience on International Drive.
transportNotes: Located at ICON Park on International Drive. Free parking in ICON Park garage.
tips: Combo tickets with Madame Tussauds and The Wheel save up to 40%
The behind-the-scenes tour offers feeding experiences and deeper learning

name: Museum of Illusions Orlando
description: Museum of Illusions Orlando at ICON Park features dozens of optical illusions, holograms, and mind-bending installations designed for interactive photos and social media content. The museum challenges perception and provides entertaining photo opportunities.
transportNotes: Located at ICON Park on International Drive. Free parking.
tips: Bring your phone fully charged - every exhibit is designed for photos
Allow about 60-90 minutes for a full visit

name: Nona Adventure Park
description: Nona Adventure Park in Lake Nona is an outdoor water and adventure park featuring a cable-driven wake park, 60-foot climbing tower, and a floating aqua park on a lake. The venue offers a unique active outdoor experience away from the theme parks.
transportNotes: Located in the Lake Nona community in southeast Orlando. Free parking.
tips: The aqua park (floating obstacle course) is incredibly fun and challenging
No experience needed for the cable-driven wake park - instructors teach you

name: Magical Midway Thrill Park
description: Magical Midway is a roadside amusement park on International Drive featuring elevated go-kart tracks, the Sling Shot thrill ride, bungee jumping, and arcade games. The park has been an I-Drive staple since the 1990s.
transportNotes: Located on International Drive. Free parking in lot.
tips: The elevated go-kart track offers great views of I-Drive
The Sling Shot ($25) launches you 390 feet in the air at 100 mph

name: Pirate's Cove Adventure Golf
description: Pirate's Cove Adventure Golf on International Drive features two 18-hole pirate-themed miniature golf courses with waterfalls, caves, and pirate ship theming. The Captain's Course and Blackbeard's Challenge offer different difficulty levels.
transportNotes: Located on International Drive. Free parking.
tips: Blackbeard's Challenge is the harder of the two courses
Evening play offers cooler temperatures and dramatic lighting

name: I-Drive NASCAR Indoor Kart Racing
description: I-Drive NASCAR is an indoor go-kart racing experience on International Drive featuring electric karts on a NASCAR-themed track with electronic timing and competitive racing formats. The venue also includes an arcade and VR racing simulators.
transportNotes: Located on International Drive. Free parking.
tips: The electric karts are faster than they look - expect a real racing experience
Multi-race packages offer better per-race pricing

name: Volcano Island Mini Golf
description: Volcano Island Mini Golf near ICON Park features an 18-hole tropical volcano-themed course with waterfalls, lava flows, and erupting volcano effects. The compact course is well-themed and conveniently located on the I-Drive entertainment strip.
transportNotes: Located on International Drive near ICON Park. Free parking.
tips: The volcano eruption effects make evening play especially fun
Conveniently located near ICON Park for a combined evening out

name: Bonanza Golf
description: Bonanza Golf on International Drive features two 18-hole courses with dinosaur and Western themes. The courses offer a budget-friendly mini golf experience compared to the more elaborate themed options on I-Drive.
transportNotes: Located on International Drive. Free parking.
tips: One of the most affordable mini golf options on I-Drive
The dinosaur statues throughout make for fun photo ops

name: Gator Spot at Fun Spot
description: Gator Spot at Fun Spot Kissimmee is an alligator and wildlife exhibit featuring Florida native animals including alligators, tortoises, and exotic birds. The exhibit is located within the Fun Spot Kissimmee park.
transportNotes: Located at Fun Spot Kissimmee on US-192. Free parking.
tips: Gator Spot is included with Fun Spot Kissimmee admission
Alligator feedings happen at scheduled times throughout the day

name: Boomers Boca Raton
description: Boomers in Boca Raton is a family entertainment park with go-kart racing, mini golf, bumper boats, batting cages, and a large arcade. The park offers an affordable family outing in the Boca Raton area.
transportNotes: Located near Boca Raton Airport. Free parking.
tips: All-day wristbands offer the best value for families spending several hours
The go-kart track is one of the best in Palm Beach County

name: Sleuth's Mystery Dinner Shows
description: Sleuth's Mystery Dinner Shows on International Drive combines a murder mystery comedy show with a three-course dinner. Guests play detective as they watch the mystery unfold, question suspects, and try to solve the crime before dessert.
transportNotes: Located on International Drive. Free parking.
tips: The mystery changes regularly so repeat visits offer new experiences
Audience participation is key to the fun - do not be shy

name: Mango's Tropical Cafe Orlando
description: Mango's Tropical Cafe on International Drive brings the energy of Miami's famous South Beach nightclub to Orlando. The venue features live music, Latin dancing performances, a Caribbean-inspired dinner menu, and a high-energy atmosphere with performers dancing on the bar and throughout the venue.
transportNotes: Located on International Drive. Free parking in lot.
tips: The dinner show starts around 7 PM with performances throughout the evening
The dance floor opens up after dinner for guest dancing

name: Outta Control Magic Comedy Dinner Show
description: The Outta Control Magic Comedy Dinner Show at WonderWorks combines magic, comedy, and improv with unlimited pizza, salad, beer, wine, and soft drinks. The 90-minute show is fast-paced and family-friendly with heavy audience participation.
transportNotes: Located at WonderWorks on International Drive. Free parking.
tips: Unlimited pizza, salad, beer, wine, and soft drinks are included in the ticket price
The show is very interactive - sit up front if you want to be involved

name: Cirque du Soleil - Drawn to Life
address: 1478 E Buena Vista Dr, Orlando, FL 32830
description: Drawn to Life is Disney's exclusive Cirque du Soleil show at Disney Springs, combining acrobatic artistry with Disney animation in a touching story about a girl who discovers a series of unfinished drawings by her late father, a Disney animator.
transportNotes: Located at Disney Springs West Side. Free parking in Disney Springs garages.
tips: Shows run Tuesday through Saturday with matinee and evening performances
Category 1 and 2 seats offer the best views of the aerial performances

name: Blue Man Group at Universal Orlando
description: Blue Man Group performs at their dedicated theater at Universal CityWalk. The high-energy show combines percussion, comedy, multimedia, and audience participation in a one-of-a-kind performance that defies categorization.
transportNotes: Located at Universal CityWalk. Universal parking $30 (free after 6 PM).
tips: The poncho zone seats in the first few rows may get splattered
CityWalk parking is free after 6 PM for evening shows

name: Spirit of Aloha Dinner Show
description: The Spirit of Aloha Dinner Show at Disney's Polynesian Village Resort is a traditional Hawaiian luau featuring Polynesian dancers, fire knife performers, live music, and an all-you-can-eat dinner of island-inspired cuisine. The open-air theater overlooks Seven Seas Lagoon.
transportNotes: Located at Disney's Polynesian Village Resort. Monorail from Magic Kingdom or car with resort parking.
tips: Category 1 seating offers the closest views of the performers
The fire knife finale is spectacular - a must-see Disney experience

name: Hoop-Dee-Doo Musical Revue
address: 4510 N Fort Wilderness Trail, Orlando, FL 32830
description: Hoop-Dee-Doo Musical Revue at Disney's Fort Wilderness Resort is Disney's longest-running dinner show (since 1974). The Old West-themed variety show features singing, dancing, comedy, and an all-you-can-eat feast of fried chicken, ribs, cornbread, and strawberry shortcake.
transportNotes: Located at Pioneer Hall in Disney's Fort Wilderness Resort. Boat from Magic Kingdom or bus service.
tips: One of Disney's most beloved and longest-running shows since 1974
The all-you-can-eat ribs and fried chicken are hearty and delicious

name: Arabian Nights Dinner Attraction
description: Arabian Nights is a dinner show featuring world-class equestrian performances with over 30 horses performing dressage, liberty acts, and trick riding. The show takes place in a large indoor arena while guests enjoy a multi-course dinner.
transportNotes: Located in Kissimmee near US-192. Free parking.
tips: Horse lovers will be captivated by the variety of breeds and performance styles
VIP seating offers closer views of the horses and performers

name: Treasure Tavern
address: 5731 W Irlo Bronson Hwy, Kissimmee, FL 34746
description: Treasure Tavern is an adults-only (18+) dinner show featuring a pirate-themed variety performance with acrobatics, aerial acts, comedy, and a four-course dinner with premium open bar. The show is more mature and sophisticated than family-oriented dinner attractions.
transportNotes: Located on US-192 in Kissimmee. Free parking.
tips: Adults-only (18+) - a more sophisticated dinner show option
The open bar is included in the ticket price making it excellent value
